Dado que los cubanos están pasando por enormes dificultades, este impasse no puede prolongarse indefinidamente. La mejor opción para La Habana es llevar a cabo reformas significativas, al tiempo que deja claro a Washington los peligros que entraña una intervención.

items.title: FG intervenes to avert strike, restore services at Futa Teaching Hospital, Ondo
items.description: By Dayo Johnson Akure The Federal Ministry of Health and Social Welfare has intervened in the industrial dispute at the Federal University of Technology Teaching Hospital, FUTATH, Akure, by inviting organised labour to a high-level meeting aimed at restoring industrial harmony. The intervention comes after the Nigeria Labour Congress, NLC, and Trade Union Congress, TUC, in Ondo State issued a seven-day strike ultimatum to the hospital management over alleged breach of the transition agreement and violations of workers’ rights. In a letter jointly signed by Ondo State NLC Chairman, Comrade Prince Ademola Olapade, and TUC Chairman, Comrade Clement Fatuase, and addressed to the Chief Medical Director, CMD, Prof. Olusegun Ojo, labour accused management of violating the Memorandum of Understanding, MoU, governing the transition of the former State Specialist Hospital to FUTATH. The unions said the ultimatum followed resolutions reached at a strategic meeting of organised labour and health sector unions in FUTATH on July 16, warning that the alleged actions threaten industrial peace. In a swift response, the Ministry, through Deputy Director, RLDO, Mr. Festus Igbinosun, notified the TUC Chairman that a delegation would visit the hospital on Friday, July 24, 2026 to address issues surrounding the disruption of services and other matters affecting the hospital’s operations. The meeting is scheduled to hold at the Boardroom of FUTATH. It follows a report submitted by the hospital management to the Ministry on recurring industrial actions. According to the letter, the intervention comes shortly after the Federal Government completed the transition programme for the former UNIMED Teaching Hospital, Akure, now FUTATH. The Ministry said the transition culminated in the biometric capture of all eligible staff selected into the approved Nominal Roll, in line with the MoU signed between the Ondo State Government and the Federal Ministry of Health and Social Welfare on Oct. 23, 2025. Recall that staff absorption was conducted under Article 5 (5.1.3) of the MoU, which stipulates that only eligible officers of the old hospital would be absorbed into the Federal Civil Service. Also, an inter-ministerial verification exercise was carried out between Dec. 8 and Dec. 12, 2025 by officials from the Ministries of Health and Finance, the Office of the Head of Civil Service of the Federation, and the Office of the Accountant-General of the Federation. The list of eligible officers was further scrutinised based on age, qualifications, service records, the approved structure for Federal Teaching Hospitals, and the prescribed clinical-to-non-clinical staff ratio. The Ministry said 265 members of staff of the former hospital were found ineligible for absorption and have been recommended to the Ondo State Government for redeployment into the State Civil Service. To conclude the process, biometric capture for payroll enrolment under the Integrated Personnel and Payroll Information System, IPPIS, was conducted in Akure between June 8 you and June 13, 2026. A mop-up exercise was held in Abuja from July 9 to July 13, 2026 for officers who missed the earlier exercise. The Coordinating Minister of Health and Social Welfare, Prof. Muhammad Ali Pate, commended the Ondo State Government and the people of the state for their cooperation, describing the successful takeover as a product of mutual understanding and collaboration. Meanwhile, stakeholders in the state have expressed optimism that the engagement with organised labour would resolve outstanding issues and strengthen industrial peace, enabling the hospital to focus on healthcare delivery, teaching and research. The post FG intervenes to avert strike, restore services at Futa Teaching Hospital, Ondo appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Gov Yusuf inaugurates multi-agency task force on drug abuse
items.description: … Signs Executive Order against thuggery in Kano By Dapo Akinrefon Governor Abba Kabir Yusuf of Kano State has inaugurated the Multi-Agency Task Force on Drug Abuse and signed an Executive Order aimed at curbing thuggery and related criminal activities across the state. This was contained in a statement issued by the Governor’s Spokesperson, Sunusi Bature Dawakin Tofa, on Wednesday. Governor Yusuf described drug abuse as one of the greatest threats confronting Kano’s youthful population, warning that the menace fuels violent crimes, destroys families, weakens the economy, and undermines social stability. He said the newly inaugurated Task Force would “coordinate the state’s response to drug abuse through intelligence gathering, enforcement, public enlightenment, rehabilitation, and collaboration with security agencies and other relevant stakeholders.” The Governor reaffirmed his administration’s commitment to supporting the Task Force with adequate funding, operational vehicles, office accommodation, logistics, and security backing, while guaranteeing its operational independence to enable it discharge its mandate effectively. He also reiterated his administration’s commitment to expanding rehabilitation facilities for victims of drug abuse, noting that the “state government has already established separate rehabilitation centres for male and female patients and would continue to strengthen support for their recovery and reintegration into society.” He explained that the Executive Order against thuggery would provide a stronger legal framework for dismantling criminal gangs, curbing political violence, and complementing the efforts of security agencies in maintaining law and order across Kano State. Governor Yusuf urged residents to support the Task Force by providing credible information and cooperating with security agencies, stressing that the fight against drug abuse and thuggery requires the collective efforts of government and the public. He expressed confidence that the combined implementation of the Executive Order and the activities of the Multi-Agency Task Force would significantly reduce drug-related crimes and contribute to a safer, more peaceful, and prosperous Kano State. In his remarks, the Chairman of the Task Force, Mr Muhuyi Magaji Rimingado, thanked Governor Yusuf for the confidence reposed in him and other members of the committee. He assured the Governor and the people of Kano that the Task Force would “carry out its assignment with integrity, professionalism, and without compromise.” Muhuyi said members of the Task Force fully understand the enormity of their responsibility and would work “closely with security agencies, traditional rulers, religious leaders, civil society organisations, educational institutions, and community leaders to combat drug abuse and promote lasting peace across the state.” He described the establishment of the Task Force as a demonstration of Governor Yusuf’s determination to safeguard the future of Kano’s youth and strengthen public safety. The post Gov Yusuf inaugurates multi-agency task force on drug abuse appeared first on Vanguard News .

Tres meses después del arranque de la vista , su abogado ha expuesto al tribunal su alegato final y ha aprovechado para desmarcarlo de la puesta en marcha de la operación de espionaje al extesorero popular Luis Bárcenas, que tenía como supuesto objetivo robarle documentos comprometedores que pudiera guardar de cargos del partido para que no llegaran así a los investigadores del caso Gürtel . La defensa ha reprochado que se trata de endosar al exdirigente del PP una “responsabilidad penal” por el mero hecho de que ejercía el cargo de ministro; y ha cargado contra Francisco Martínez, ex secretario de Estado de Seguridad y su antigua mano derecha, que implicó a su exjefe en la trama durante la fase de instrucción. items.title: CAN 2027 : la CAF officialise le passage de 24 à 28 équipes
items.description: La Coupe d’Afrique des nations va changer de dimension. Le président de la Confédération africaine de football (CAF), Patrice Motsepe, a annoncé ce mercredi 22 juillet 2026, lors d’une conférence de presse à Johannesburg, que la CAN 2027 réunira 28 sélections, contre 24 lors des précédentes éditions. Cette réforme entrera donc en vigueur dès la CAN Pamoja 2027, organisée conjointement par le Kenya, la Tanzanie et l’Ouganda, du 19 juin au 17 juillet 2027. « Nous avons augmenté le nombre de participants à la prochaine édition de la CAN » , a déclaré Patrice Motsepe, officialisant ainsi l’élargissement du tournoi continental. Le passage de 24 à 28 participants entraînera nécessairement une modification du format de la compétition. Avec quatre équipes supplémentaires, la phase de groupes devrait désormais compter sept poules de quatre sélections. Pour constituer le tableau des huitièmes de finale, les deux premiers de chacun des sept groupes, soit 14 équipes, pourraient être accompagnés des deux meilleurs troisièmes, portant à 16 le nombre de qualifiés pour la phase à élimination directe. Ce format reste toutefois à préciser officiellement dans ses modalités détaillées par la CAF. Lors du précédent format à 24 équipes, les sélections étaient réparties en six groupes de quatre. Les deux premiers de chaque poule ainsi que les quatre meilleurs troisièmes décrochaient leur billet pour les huitièmes de finale. Les qualifications pour la CAN 2027 doivent se dérouler sur trois fenêtres internationales : les deux premières journées entre le 21 septembre et le 6 octobre 2026, les troisième et quatrième journées du 9 au 17 novembre 2026, puis les deux dernières journées du 22 au 30 mars 2027. Champion d’Afrique en titre, le Maroc évoluera dans le groupe A des éliminatoires, aux côtés du Gabon, du Niger et du Lesotho. Les Lions de l’Atlas tenteront de décrocher leur qualification pour cette première CAN de l’histoire disputée à 28 équipes. The post CAN 2027 : la CAF officialise le passage de 24 à 28 équipes appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: Anti-Tinubu Post: Court rejects Sowore’s uncertified exhibits
items.description: …. as trial continues July 28 By Ikechukwu Nnochiri ABUJA — Trial of the presidential candidate of the African Action Congress (AAC), Omoyele Sowore, continued on Wednesday with the Federal High Court in Abuja rejecting a document he sought to tender in defence of the cybercrime charges the Department of State Services (DSS) filed against him. The security agency is prosecuting him on a two-count charge for calling President Bola Tinubu a “criminal” in a post he shared on social media platforms. At the resumed proceedings, the politician and activist sought to tender some documents in evidence through his third witness. In an application made through his team of lawyers led by Mr. Olumide Fusika, SAN, Sowore disclosed that the documents he sought to have admitted in evidence contained a summary of proceedings featuring the testimony of a witness of the DSS codenamed XYZ. The record of proceedings was accompanied by letters the defendant urged the court to admit in evidence to strengthen his defence. He moved to tender the exhibits through a lawyer, Wayne Chikezie Elijah, who appeared before the court as a defence witness. However, counsel to the DSS, Mr. Akinlolu Kehinde, SAN, challenged the admissibility of the documents, which he noted were not certified. The prosecution counsel contended that since the exhibits contained public documents, they ought to be properly certified to meet the admissibility threshold. He therefore urged the court to reject the documents for the defendant’s failure to comply with provisions of the Evidence Act. Despite the argument of Sowore’s lawyer that the documents, which he said came from the court’s records, needed no certification, he was overruled by trial Justice Mohammed Umar. Justice Umar held that the documents came from the custody of a counsel who claimed he had earlier appeared for the defendant in the matter. “The reason for certification is to be sure the document has not been tampered with. Having been in the custody of the said counsel, how is the court sure that the document has not been tampered with?” Justice Umar queried. He refused the defendant’s application and marked the documents as rejected. Earlier in the proceedings, another witness, Mr. Uwen Davis, who identified himself as a Principal Officer, General Services, in the DSS, testified before the court. The witness told the court that he authored a letter dated September 7, 2025, and delivered it to the Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of “X Corporation,” requesting that the offending post Sowore made against President Tinubu be deleted. Davis told the court that part of the reasons adduced in the letter was that Sowore’s post was capable of leading to a breakdown of law and order, considering that President Tinubu’s supporters were becoming infuriated. Asked to name some of the supporters who were getting furious over the alleged offensive post, the witness said he could not specifically name anyone. He, however, admitted that he wrote the letter based on instructions from his superior officer. Justice Umar fixed July 28 for the continuation of the hearing in the case. Sowore had, on December 2, 2025, pleaded not guilty to the charge marked FHC/ABJ/CR/484/2025, filed against him by the DSS. The charges allege offences under Sections 24(1)(b) and 24(2)(a), (b), and (c) of the Cybercrimes (Prohibition, Prevention, Etc.) (Amendment) Act, 2024. The offending posts, made on August 25, 2025, were in response to President Tinubu’s claim, made in Brazil, that his administration had ended corruption in Nigeria. Angered by the posts, the DSS demanded that X Inc. (formerly Twitter) and Meta Platforms Inc. ban Sowore’s accounts and remove the posts. The security agency also wrote to Sowore, asking him to delete the posts from all platforms. Non-compliance with the request led to the charges. The prosecution claims the defamatory posts were intended to cause a breakdown of law and order and to tarnish the president’s reputation. Exhibits include printouts of the posts and the DSS letters. X Inc. and Meta were initially co-defendants but were delisted in the amended charge. The post Anti-Tinubu Post: Court rejects Sowore’s uncertified exhibits appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Accra : Bourita représente le Roi au Sommet extraordinaire de l'UA sur l'élimination du Sida d'ici 2030
items.description: Sur Très Haute Instruction Royale, le ministre des Affaires étrangères, de la Coopération africaine et des Marocains résidant à l’étranger, Nasser Bourita, représente le Roi Mohammed VI au Sommet extraordinaire de l’Union Africaine sur l’élimination du Sida et la lutte contre les décès maternels évitables, maladies transmissibles et non transmissibles endémiques, et le renforcement des systèmes de santé à l’horizon 2030, qui se tient mercredi à Accra. A cette occasion, Bourita a mis en avant la Vision Royale d’une souveraineté sanitaire africaine fondée sur la solidarité, la justice sociale et le partage d’expertises. Il a souligné, notamment, que le Maroc, sous la conduite du Roi Mohammed VI, que Dieu L’assiste, a fait de la santé un pilier stratégique de sa coopération continentale, en phase avec les trois grands enjeux du Sommet : la fabrication locale de produits médicaux, le financement innovant et la gouvernance politique. Axée sur l'élimination du Sida à l'horizon 2030 et la lutte contre les décès maternels évitables, les maladies transmissibles et non transmissibles endémiques sur le continent, ainsi que sur le renforcement des systèmes de santé à l'horizon 2030, ce Sommet intervient dans la suite de la décision de la 38e session ordinaire de la Conférence des Chefs d'État et de gouvernement relative à l'évolution des priorités sanitaires de l'Afrique. Le Sommet a examiné une feuille de route de l'UA, à l'horizon 2030 et au-delà, afin de pérenniser la riposte au VIH/Sida, de renforcer la résilience des systèmes de santé et d'améliorer la sécurité sanitaire sur l'ensemble du continent. La délégation marocaine, conduite par le ministre des Affaires étrangères, de la Coopération africaine et des Marocains résidant à l’étranger, est composée de représentants du ministère de la Santé et du ministère de l’Economie et des Finances. The post Accra : Bourita représente le Roi au Sommet extraordinaire de l'UA sur l'élimination du Sida d'ici 2030 appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: Hitler's Austrian birthplace transformed into police station
items.description: Austria opened a new police station in Adolf Hitler’s former house on Wednesday, in the hope of deterring today’s ascendant far-right extremists from making the pilgrimage to the Nazi dictator’s birthplace. In a ceremony to mark the opening, Johannes Waidbacher, mayor of the small town of Braunau-am-Inn, said he hoped the transformation would show that the local community is “confronting its history, that it is assuming responsibility”. The decision to turn the house into a police station follows a long and delicate debate over what to do with the site, in a country often criticised for not taking full responsibility for its role in the Holocaust. A police priest hands over a blessed cross to police officers during an inauguration ceremony of the birth house of former German dictator Adolf Hitler that is turned into a police station, on July 22, 2026 in Braunau am Inn, Austria. — AFP “I believe that the opening of the police station today brings clarity to the discussion, perhaps also a bit of calm for the people of Braunau,” the mayor said, in a ceremony also attended by Interior Minister Gerhard Karner. Few local residents were present, but 90-year-old Erna Seidl said she was “relieved” that at last “something” had been done and “very happy with the result”. After a €20 million ($23m) refurbishment finished three years behind schedule, the unassuming building where one of history’s most notorious figures was born is all but unrecognisable. ‘Place of democracy’ The roughcast yellow walls of the 17th-century home near the German border, where the future dictator was born on April 20, 1889, have been whitewashed over by the Austrian architecture firm Marte. A police sign hangs above the entrance, while the pavement still bears a memorial stone with the inscription: “For Peace, Freedom and Democracy. Never Again Fascism. Millions of Dead Warn.” Interior Minister Karner said that from now on the house would be “a place of democracy, of the rule of law, of security, of freedom, and of human dignity”. This combination of pictures created on July 22, 2026 shows Adolf Hitler’s birthplace, photographed on September 20, 2012, in Braunau, Austria and (R) Adolf Hitler’s birthplace in Braunau, Austria, photographed on July 22, 2026. — AFP “And at the same time … the memory does not disappear, nothing is covered up. The memorial stone in front of this house, hewn from the granite of the former Mauthausen concentration camp, remains in place,” he said. After Hitler’s Germany annexed its Alpine neighbour in 1938, 65,000 Austrian Jews were killed and another 130,000 forced into exile. But the country’s most popular political party is now the far-right FPOe, which was founded by former Nazis. Around 37 per cent of Austrians back the party, according to polling aggregator Europe Elects. The house, which had been in the hands of the same family from 1912, was from 1972 leased to the Austrian state and used as a centre for disabled people, who were also among the Third Reich’s victims. Despite neo-Nazis frequently flocking to the address, its last private owner, Gerlinde Pommer, vetoed any transformation of the house and contested its expropriation by the state until the very end. Three years after Austria passed a law in 2016 to take control of the dilapidated building, the Supreme Court finally approved the ­€810,000 purchase of the 800 square-metre property. ‘Humanity will not forget’ Demolishing the site was out of the question for historians. They urged Austria to face up to its role in the Nazis’ murder of more than six million Jews, along with Roma, LGBTQ people and communists. An expert commission decided to make it a police station, with the Austrian government insisting that would “clearly show” that no commemoration of Nazism would be acceptable and would “neutralise” the site. Austria’s Interior Minister Gerhard Karner speaks during an inauguration ceremony of the birth house of former German dictator Adolf Hitler that is turned into a police station, on July 22, 2026 in Braunau am Inn, Austria. — AFP “But that’s absurd: humanity will not forget where the worst mass murderer in history was born,” Robert Eiter, spokesman for the Mauthausen Committee Austria, an association of escapees from the concentration camp, told AFP . “And this renovation will not prevent a single neo-Nazi from making the pilgrimage to Braunau.” Former Interior Minister Wolfgang Peschorn, in office at the time of the decision, said Wednesday that putting a police station “tasked with … safeguarding the rule of law” in the house was “the clearest sign of a democracy”. Andreas Pilsl, head of the regional police in Upper Austria, said he believed that “the officers here are very much aware of where they are working and of the context”. But Florian Kotanko of Braunau’s Association for Contemporary History said he doubted the conversion would end the controversy. “Braunau will always be associated around the world with the town where Adolf Hitler was born … This is not the end of the story,” he told AFP .

items.title: Ioba : Au total, 217 professeurs des écoles à la conquête du CSAPé, session 2026
items.description: Burkina/Ioba-Examen-Professionnel Ioba : Au total, 217 professeurs des écoles à la conquête du CSAPé, session 2026 Dano, 21 juillet 2026 (AIB) – Le haut-commissaire de la province du Ioba, Innocents Ouattara, a donné le top de départ de l’examen du Certificat supérieur d’aptitude pédagogique (CSAPé), session de 2026 à Dano le mardi 21 juillet 2026. Le haut-commissaire, Innocents Ouattara, était accompagné par le Directeur provincial de l’éducation préscolaire, primaire et non formelle (DPEPPNF) du Ioba, Tibouo-ion Romaric Somé, des autorités administratives et sécuritaires. Les 217 candidats inscrits dans la province du Ioba pour la présente session de l’examen du CSAPé composent à l’école primaire publique Moutori ‘’A’’ de Dano. Le premier responsable de la province a officiellement lancé cet examen professionnel avec l’ouverture de l’enveloppe de l’épreuve théorique. Cette épreuve est composée d’une étude de cas et d’une fiche pédagogique. Avant de donner le top de départ de l’examen, le haut-commissaire et sa délégation ont fait le tour des salles de composition pour encourager les candidats. « Il faut rester discipliné et concentré sur les sujets. Vous aspirez à devenir de cadres supérieurs alors faites une bonne composition. Au regard des mines qui se présentent ce matin, je suis rassuré que le Ioba réalisera un taux de réussite de 100%. Je vous encourage à bien travailler », a conseillé M. Ouattara. Les candidats à l’examen du CSAPé sont des professeurs des écoles (PE). Après leur admission définitive, à l’issue de la pratique de classe, ils seront des professeurs certifiés des écoles (PCE). Agence d’information du Burkina SZ-DNK

items.title: GPHA kickstarts 40th anniversary celebration with health and awareness walks
items.description: The Ghana Ports and Harbours Authority (GPHA) on Saturday, July 8 2026, commenced activities marking its 40th Anniversary celebration with colourful health and awareness walks in both Tema and Takoradi. The walks, led by the Directors of Port for Tema and Takoradi, Tebon Zumah Esq. and Captain Richmond Quayeson respectively, brought together management, staff, retirees, families, union executives and the wider GPHA community in a vibrant display of unity, pride and togetherness. With music, dancing, chants and GPHA’s iconic blue, yellow and white prominently on display, the event promoted healthy living while celebrating the strong sense of community that has sustained the Authority through four decades of dedicated service to Ghana’s maritime and trade sectors. Addressing participants at the Junior Club House in Tema Community Two (2) after the walk, the Director of Port commended staff and retirees for their enthusiasm and resilience throughout the exercise. He expressed appreciation to the entire GPHA family for their dedication and commitment over the past four decades, saying their collective efforts have helped sustain public confidence in Ghana’s ports and the country’s trade facilitation agenda. The Director of Port announced that management is considering making the health walk a regular feature on the Authority’s calendar, citing its physical, mental and social benefits for staff. He also disclosed plans to establish a dedicated sports centre to provide employees with a permanent facility for recreational and wellness activities, including fitness programmes, sporting events and other staff engagement initiatives. Tebon Zumah urged employees to remain committed to excellence in their respective roles to ensure the Ports of Tema and Takoradi continue to strengthen their reputation as efficient and competitive gateways for trade in the sub-region. In Takoradi, the Director of Port of Takoradi, Captain James Richmond Quayson described the anniversary as a celebration of the merger that birthed the modern GPHA and lauded the authority’s evolution into a major shipping and logistics hub. He praised the commitment of Team Takoradi to efficient service delivery, stressing that every department is driven by the shared goal of getting the job done. He wished staff continued success and prayed for blessings upon employees, management and the board.

items.title: Intermittent heavy rain hits Punjab cities, Lahore receives 123mm
items.description: Intermittent heavy rainfall lashed several cities in Punjab over the previous 24 hours, the Provincial Disaster Management Authority (PDMA) said on Wednesday. According to the Water and Sanitation Agency (Wasa), 123 millimetres of rain was recorded in Lahore, a record 245mm in Gujranwala and 95mm in Gujrat. The PDMA said 139mm of rainfall was recorded in Sialkot, 138mm in Mandi Bahauddin, 117mm in Narowal, 85mm in Hafizabad, 70mm in Chakwal, 64mm in Okara, 35mm in Jhang, 33mm in Layyah and 32mm in Jhelum. The disaster management authority further said that since the start of the monsoon season in the province, at least 17 people have died in rain-related incidents. Of the reported deaths, 12 were caused by the collapse of buildings or other structures, two by lightning strikes and three by electrocution, the PDMA said. Meanwhile, CM Maryam also appealed to the public not to risk their lives by going near or bathing in canals, rivers and rainwater drains during the monsoon season and flood conditions. Residents faced hardships due to accumulated rainwater on roads in major cities of central and northern Punjab. Photos shared on social media and corroborated by a Dawn correspondent showed the Shamsi Underpass in Gujranwala’s Civil Lines area completely submerged on Wednesday, making it impossible for traffic to pass through. Rainwater accumulated in different areas of Lahore, including Kareem Block Market, Johar Town, Township, Ferozepur Road, Multan Road, Samanabad, Gulshan-i-Ravi, Lakshmi Chowk, Model Town and other major roads and streets across the city. Several photos and videos shared on social media showed roads inundated in Lahore, leaving commuters stranded. Videos posted on social media platform X showed cars wading through knee-deep water on major roads across the city. Social media users also reported prolonged power outages in areas that received rainfall over the past 24 hours. However, Wasa Lahore claimed that no water accumulation was reported on most major roads and intersections, according to a statement. The statement added that real-time monitoring of citywide drainage operations was being conducted from the monsoon control room. “Teams are mobilised for immediate cleaning of main drains and channels to provide relief to citizens,” stated DG Wasa Punjab, while conducting field monitoring in Gujranwala. According to Wasa, rainwater drainage efforts were underway in 41 districts, while authorities are continuously monitoring rivers, dams and barrages. All Punjab Wasa field teams are on high alert, with directives issued to ensure the swiftest possible drainage of rainwater, Wasa Punjab director general said, adding that public convenience was the top priority. He further advised citizens to avoid unnecessary travel and contact the relevant authorities in case of an emergency. In Gujrat, Wasa said 20 of its emergency teams remained continuously engaged in drainage operations across the city’s three zones. Traffic flowed normally on major roads, Wasa Gujrat claimed. Punjab’s Housing Minister Bilal Yasin directed all departments to remain mobilised to promptly address difficulties faced by citizens during the monsoon season. He advised prioritising drainage of water from low-lying areas. “With above-normal rainfall expected during this monsoon season, the drainage system across Punjab — including Lahore — is being made more effective and modernised,” the provincial minister said.

items.title: Gestion des biens de Sidiki Mara, disparu : Aissatou Kenda Bah relaxée, sa coépouse Maimouna Bah condamnée à lui verser 10 millions GNF…
items.description: Le tribunal de première instance de Dixinn a rendu, ce mercredi 22 juillet 2026, son verdict dans l’affaire opposant Maimouna Bah à sa coépouse, Aissatou Kenda Bah. La première poursuivait la seconde pour des faits présumés de faux et usage de faux dans le cadre de la gestion des biens de leur époux, Elhadj Sidiki Mara, promoteur immobilier et propriétaire du groupe Sankara Holdings, disparu depuis décembre dernier et dont la famille affirme être toujours sans nouvelles. Dans cette affaire, Aissatou Kenda Bah était accusée d’avoir falsifié une procuration afin de gérer le patrimoine immobilier de son époux, porté disparu depuis décembre 2025 et dont aucune nouvelle n’a été enregistrée depuis. À l’ouverture des débats, le 7 mai dernier, Hadja Mara, fille d’Elhadj Sidiki Mara et de Maimouna Bah, a déclaré avoir découvert l’existence de cette procuration après la disparition de son père. « Elle utilise, selon moi, un faux document pour contrôler les biens de mon père. Ce document aurait été établi en 2009, mais elle ne l’a présenté qu’après la disparition de mon père, ce qui paraît suspect. […] Je connais Aissatou Kenda Bah comme étant la petite amie de mon père », avait-elle déclaré. Au cours des audiences, Aissatou Kenda Bah a rejeté les accusations portées contre elle. Selon elle, la procuration qu’elle détient est authentique et lui a été délivrée par son époux à Paris avant sa disparition. « Cette procuration m’a été remise par mon époux. Elle a été établie à Paris, en mon absence, le 10 mars 2009, par mon époux Elhadj Sidiki Mara afin que je gère ses biens lorsqu’il ne sera plus là, car il me fait confiance. Lorsqu’il me l’a envoyée, j’en ai transmis une copie au notaire, qui a accompli les formalités nécessaires », a-t-elle expliqué. De son côté, l’avocat de la partie civile, Me Amadou DS Bah, a contesté la validité de cette procuration et demandé au tribunal de saisir l’Ordre des notaires de Paris afin de vérifier l’authenticité du document présenté par Aissatou Kenda Bah. Lors des plaidoiries et réquisitions, le lundi dernier, le représentant du ministère public avait requis la relaxe de la prévenue pour les faits de faux et usage de faux. Pour sa part, l’avocat de la défense a sollicité le rejet de la constitution de partie civile de Maimouna Bah et de sa fille. Au titre des dommages et intérêts en réparation du préjudice qu’elle estimait avoir subi, la défense a réclamé le paiement de 500 millions de francs guinéens. Dans son jugement rendu ce mercredi 22 juillet 2026, le président du tribunal de première instance de Dixinn, Aboubacar Thiam, a renvoyé Aissatou Kenda Bah des fins de la poursuite, estimant que les délits poursuivis n’étaient pas constitués. Le tribunal a également condamné la plaignante, Maimouna Bah, à verser 10 millions de francs guinéens à titre de dommages et intérêts à Aissatou Kenda Bah. Mamadou Yaya BARRY L’article Gestion des biens de Sidiki Mara, disparu : Aissatou Kenda Bah relaxée, sa coépouse Maimouna Bah condamnée à lui verser 10 millions GNF… est apparu en premier sur Mediaguinee.com .

items.title: France: les réseaux sociaux interdits aux moins de 15 ans dès la rentrée
items.description: La France devient le premier pays européen à adopter une interdiction des réseaux sociaux pour les moins de 15 ans. Votée définitivement par le Parlement le 21 juillet 2026, cette réforme, voulue par le président Emmanuel Macron, vise à mieux protéger les mineurs face aux risques liés au numérique. Le Parlement français a définitivement adopté, mardi 21 juillet 2026, une loi interdisant l'accès aux réseaux sociaux aux enfants de moins de 15 ans. Le texte a été approuvé par une large majorité dans les deux chambres: 243 sénateurs ont voté en sa faveur, tandis que l'Assemblée nationale l'a validé par 279 voix contre 81. À l'issue du vote, le président Emmanuel Macron s'est félicité de cette adoption, sur le réseau social X. Il a rappelé qu'il s'agissait d'un engagement de son mandat et a indiqué attendre désormais la décision du Conseil constitutionnel avant la mise en œuvre effective de la mesure. Le chef de l'État a insisté sur la nécessité de renforcer la protection des enfants dans l'univers numérique. Selon la ministre déléguée au Numérique, Anne Le Hénanff, l'interdiction s'appliquera à partir du 1er septembre 2026 pour toute création de nouveaux comptes. Les comptes déjà existants devront, quant à eux, se conformer à la nouvelle réglementation à compter du 1er janvier 2027. Cette décision n'a toutefois pas fait l'unanimité. Plusieurs élus de gauche, notamment ceux de La France insoumise, se sont opposés au texte, estimant que son application pratique soulève de nombreuses interrogations, notamment en matière de contrôle de l'âge des utilisateurs. Avec cette loi, la France devient le premier État de l'Union européenne à instaurer une telle interdiction. Elle emboîte le pas à l'Australie, qui avait adopté une mesure similaire pour les moins de 16 ans en décembre 2025. D'autres pays européens, dont l'Autriche, le Danemark, la Grèce et le Royaume-Uni, réfléchissent également à renforcer l'encadrement de l'accès des mineurs aux réseaux sociaux. En attendant la validation du Conseil constitutionnel, cette réforme marque un tournant dans la politique française de protection des mineurs en ligne et pourrait inspirer d'autres pays européens confrontés aux mêmes enjeux liés à l'utilisation des réseaux sociaux par les plus jeunes. Par Valentin SOMANDE L’article France: les réseaux sociaux interdits aux moins de 15 ans dès la rentrée est apparu en premier sur Wakat Séra .

items.title: Pabré : Une centrale de 50 MW inaugurée
items.description: Le Premier ministre Rimtalba Jean Emmanuel Ouédraogo, a procédé, le mardi 21 juillet 2026 à Pabré, à l'inauguration officielle de la centrale thermique d'urgence d'une capacité de 50 mégawatts. Réalisée en seulement 90 jours, contre un délai contractuel de 120 jours, cette infrastructure, d'un coût de 18 milliards de FCFA, vient renforcer les capacités de production de la Société nationale d'électricité du Burkina (SONABEL) et contribuer à l'amélioration de l'offre énergétique nationale. Le Premier ministre a salué la mise en service de la centrale de Pabré, premier jalon d'un vaste programme engagé dans le cadre de la Révolution progressiste populaire, en vue d'assurer l'autonomie stratégique et la souveraineté énergétique du Burkina Faso.

items.title: S&P Global Ratings upgrades Pakistan's sovereign credit rating to 'B'
items.description: S&P Global Ratings on Wednesday upgraded Pakistan’s long-term sovereign credit rating to ‘B’ from ‘B-’, citing an improving external position and gradual macroeconomic stabilisation, while assigning a stable outlook. S&P kept Pakistan’s short-term credit rating steady at a ‘B’ while upgrading its transfer and convertibility rating to ‘B’ from a ‘B-’, signalling that Islamabad can meet its immediate debt payments and it is getting easier for foreign investors to exchange currency and move funds abroad. A country’s sovereign credit rating serves as an indicator of its financial health for foreign investors. It assesses creditworthiness — the risk involved in lending money to the country, evaluated by its ability to repay its debts. Upgrading this rating gives global lenders greater confidence in the economy, helping the country attract foreign investment and borrow funds on better terms. In a statement , the credit rating agency said the change in rating was based on its belief that the country had strengthened its institutional capacity, allowing it to implement reforms of the International Monetary Fund (IMF). “Our upgrade on Pakistan is predicated on improved institutional stability that has helped to implement critical IMF programme reforms,” it said. “These reforms have quickened fiscal consolidation and rebuilt external buffers.” It noted that the institutional settings of Pakistan had strengthened over the last two years, in particular highlighting the passage of the IMF’s Extended Fund Facility (EFF) $7 billion programme in September 2024 as “critical in restoring macroeconomic stability to the country and replenishing foreign reserves”. Pakistan has met most of the EFF programme targets “thus far”, it said, allowing for timely IMF disbursements. “A relatively stable political environment has been instrumental in this regard,” it added. The statement said that the IMF programme had boosted foreign reserves, which as of the end of last month had climbed to $25.3bn —including the central bank’s gold holdings — from a multi-year low of $6.7bn in December 2022. “This is more than sufficient to cover the government’s external principal payments of $16.4bn over the next 12 months,” it said. S&P also stated that it believed multilateral and bilateral funding, alongside continued access to commercial borrowing, would diversify Pakistan’s external funding options. Due to the government’s commitment to structural reforms, it forecast the general government deficit at four per cent of the GDP for FY27, down from close to 8pc in the “crisis years” of fiscal years 2022 and 2023. It also noted that, although the State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) had tightened monetary conditions in April amid rising inflationary pressures from the Middle East conflict, domestic interest rates remained “much lower” than in previous years. “Entrenched economic reforms will bring about a sustained period of steady growth and fiscal consolidation,” the agency said. “We anticipate sustained official financing will support Pakistan in meeting its external obligations and that the country will continue to roll over its commercial credit lines over the next 12 months.” S&P added that, if the country’s commitment to fiscal consolidation were to diminish and cause current external or fiscal indicators to deteriorate — “contrary to expectations” — it may lower its ratings. It would also view surging interest rates as a sign of domestic financing stress. On the other hand, the agency may raise its rating if fiscal and external metrics “continue to strengthen structurally”. It noted that this may happen if fiscal deficits narrow and government revenue continues to rise “while financing costs moderate, with strong expenditure controls”. “Concurrently, improvements in Pakistan’s external indicators resulting in narrow net external debt falling below 100pc of current account receipts, and gross external financing needs [declining] to less than 100pc of the sum of current account receipts and usable reserves, may see the ratings raised,” it said. Awais Ashraf, director of research at AKD Securities, said the government’s reform-oriented agenda had translated into stronger fiscal discipline through a combination of “robust tax revenue growth and prudent expenditure management”. He added that the external account had strengthened on the back of sustained growth in workers’ remittances and a continued focus on expanding IT exports. As a result, he the country’s foreign exchange reserves reached a record high of $18.4bn at the end of June, while external debt has remained broadly stable over the past four years. “Despite these notable improvements in macroeconomic fundamentals, the KSE-100 index’s current valuation does not fully reflect the strengthening economic outlook,” Ashraf said. “The last time Pakistan was upgraded to a ‘B’ rating by S&P Global, the KSE-100 was trading at 9.9x forward earnings (estimations of future profits), compared with the current valuation of 7.6x.” He added that the market was currently offering a dividend yield of nearly 7pc, significantly higher than the 5.5pc dividend yield at the time of the previous upgrade. Dividend yield measures a company’s annual dividend payments as a percentage of its current share price. Last month, British financial institution Barclays upgraded Pakistan’s dollar bonds to “overweight” after having lowered the rating the previous month, citing improved oil market prospects. In April, Fitch Ratings, one of the world’s top three agencies, affirmed Pakistan’s long-term foreign currency issuer default rating (IDR) at “B-” with a “stable outlook”. However, it highlighted that the country’s high exposure to the global energy price shock remained a key risk, particularly if it led to a sharp drop in foreign exchange reserves. Last year, S&P raised Pakistan’s sovereign credit rating to ‘B-’ from ‘CCC+’ and placed it on a “stable” outlook.

items.title: CAMES : le Professeur Djientcheu Nguemaleu distingué à la présidence du jury chirurgie
items.description: © DR Figure de référence de la neurochirurgie en Afrique, le Pr Vincent de Paul DJIENTCHEU NGUEMALEU, Professeur titulaire du CAMES à l’Université de Yaoundé I, présidera le jury de la section « Chirurgie et Spécialités chirurgicales » du 23e Concours d’agrégation de Médecine, Pharmacie, Odontostomatologie, Médecine vétérinaire et Productions animales. Une désignation qui consacre son expertise scientifique, son parcours académique et son engagement en faveur de l’excellence médicale. Le Cameroun renforce son rayonnement au sein du Conseil africain et malgache pour l’enseignement supérieur (CAMES). Le Pr Vincent de Paul DJIENTCHEU NGUEMALEU a été désigné président du jury de la section « Chirurgie et Spécialités chirurgicales » du 23e Concours d’agrégation, dont les épreuves se dérouleront entre octobre et novembre 2026, en ligne puis à Bangui, en République centrafricaine. Cette responsabilité, confiée à l’un des plus éminents neurochirurgiens du continent, témoigne de la reconnaissance dont bénéficie l’enseignant-chercheur camerounais au sein de la communauté scientifique africaine. Professeur titulaire du CAMES, il s’est imposé au fil des années par la qualité de ses travaux, son expertise clinique et son implication dans la formation des futures générations de spécialistes. Au-delà de son parcours individuel, cette nomination met en lumière la place du Cameroun dans la production des compétences médicales de haut niveau. En présidant un jury chargé d’évaluer les futurs maîtres de conférences et professeurs agrégés en chirurgie, le Pr DJIENTCHEU NGUEMALEU contribuera à garantir les standards d’excellence qui fondent la crédibilité du concours d’agrégation du CAMES, rendez-vous majeur de l’enseignement supérieur et de la recherche en Afrique francophone. Cet article CAMES : le Professeur Djientcheu Nguemaleu distingué à la présidence du jury chirurgie est apparu en premier sur Journalducameroun.com .

items.title: 2027: Sheikh warns parties against same-faith governorship tickets in Kaduna
items.description: By Ibrahim Hassan-Wuyo A Kaduna-based Islamic cleric, Sheikh Haliru Maraya, has urged political parties in Kaduna State and other religiously diverse states to avoid presenting same-faith governorship and deputy governorship candidates in the 2027 elections, saying such arrangements could undermine inclusion and social harmony. Maraya, who spoke with journalists in Kaduna, said heterogeneous states should embrace balanced leadership that reflects their religious diversity in order to promote peace, unity and a sense of belonging among all citizens. According to him, Kaduna State maintained a tradition of pairing governors and deputy governors of different faiths from 1999 until the introduction of a same-faith ticket by the immediate past administration. “Since 1999, Kaduna State has always produced governors and deputies of different religious backgrounds. That display of justice strengthened unity and gave every section of the state a sense of belonging,” he said. He recalled that during the administrations of Ahmed Makarfi, Namadi Sambo, Patrick Ibrahim Yakowa and Mukhtar Ramalan Yero, governors and their deputies professed different faiths, a development he said fostered inclusiveness and stable governance. Maraya noted that those administrations also recorded significant achievements in infrastructure development, including the construction of roads and other social amenities across many parts of the state. The cleric, who served as Special Adviser on Islamic Matters to the late Governor Patrick Yakowa and former Governor Ramalan Yero, expressed concern over the adoption of a same-faith governorship ticket during the administration of former Governor Nasir El-Rufai, arguing that many residents viewed it as inconsistent with the principles of equity and fairness. He therefore called on political parties to ensure that governorship candidates and their running mates in states with diverse religious populations come from different faiths. According to him, such an arrangement would strengthen unity, encourage equal representation and deepen public confidence in government. “This will go a long way in uniting our people and ensuring that followers of the two major religions have a sense of belonging in governance. Together, we can achieve peace, unity and progress,” he said. Maraya also advised Plateau State and other heterogeneous states to adopt similar arrangements in future elections to reinforce peaceful coexistence and mutual trust among their diverse communities. He stressed that both Islam and Christianity preach peace and justice, urging political leaders to demonstrate fairness from the formation of government. Quoting the Islamic scholar, Sheikh Ibn Taymiyyah, Maraya said: “Indeed, Allah supports a just state even if it is led by unbelievers, and He does not support an unjust state even if it is led by believers.” He said the quotation underscores that justice, rather than the religious identity of leaders, remains the foundation of good governance and societal progress. The cleric further pointed to Nasarawa and Taraba states, where governors and their deputies are of different faiths, describing the arrangement as one that has promoted inclusion, unity and peaceful coexistence. He urged politicians seeking elective offices in heterogeneous states to place justice, fairness and inclusiveness above partisan considerations in the interest of sustainable peace and development. The post 2027: Sheikh warns parties against same-faith governorship tickets in Kaduna appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Full List: Real Madrid lead clubs with most goals at 2026 World Cup
items.description: Real Madrid players finished as the highest-scoring club contingent at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, combining for 22 goals across the tournament. Despite Spain lifting the World Cup trophy after edging Argentina 1-0 in extra time in Sunday’s final, Real Madrid stars delivered the most prolific attacking displays for their respective national teams. According to FIFA’s tournament statistics released on Wednesday, France captain Kylian Mbappé was the biggest contributor, scoring 10 goals to claim the Golden Boot for the second time in his career. England midfielder Jude Bellingham added seven goals, Brazil forward Vinícius Júnior found the net four times, while Türkiye international Arda Güler chipped in with one goal to complete Real Madrid’s total of 22. Paris Saint-Germain ranked second among clubs with 15 goals scored by their players during the tournament. France winger Ousmane Dembélé led PSG’s contribution with six goals, while Bradley Barcola scored three. Ibrahim Mbaye, João Neves, Nuno Mendes, Achraf Hakimi and Fabián Ruiz each added one goal. Arsenal claimed third place with a combined 13 goals, driven largely by the performances of Kai Havertz and Bukayo Saka, who each scored three times during the competition. Bayern Munich finished fourth with 12 goals. England captain Harry Kane accounted for half of that total with six goals, while Morocco midfielder Ismael Saibari contributed three. Inter Miami, Crystal Palace, Manchester United and Sunderland were tied in fifth place, with each club’s players scoring eight goals during the tournament. Lionel Messi was responsible for all eight of Inter Miami’s goals while representing Argentina, while players from Crystal Palace, Manchester United and Sunderland collectively recorded the same total for their clubs. Clubs whose players scored the most goals at the 2026 FIFA World Cup: 1. Real Madrid – 22 goals 2. Paris Saint-Germain – 15 goals 3. Arsenal – 13 goals 4. Bayern Munich – 12 goals 5. Inter Miami – 8 goals 6. Crystal Palace – 8 goals 7. Manchester United – 8 goals 8. Sunderland – 8 goals The post Full List: Real Madrid lead clubs with most goals at 2026 World Cup appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: OPHK drone strikes kill over 20 ISWAP fighters in Lake Chad
items.description: …destroy logistics hub By Ndahi Marama, Maiduguri Fresh battle damage assessment (BDA) from Operation HADIN KAI (OPHK) has confirmed that more than 20 ISWAP fighters were neutralised in a precision drone strike conducted by the Air Component of the operation at Kila Bariya in the Southern Lake Chad axis. Intelligence sources said that the precision strike, carried out on July 21, targeted a key terrorist logistics and assembly point following credible intelligence on the presence of fighters and strategic supplies in the area. According to the latest assessment, several other terrorists sustained severe injuries, while over 30 newly acquired motorcycles, most of them brand new and intended for future terrorist operations, were destroyed in the strike. The assessment further revealed that the operation destroyed a major ISWAP food storage facility stocked with large quantities of beans and other food items allegedly confiscated or forcibly collected from farming communities across the Southern Lake Chad region. Military sources described the strike as one of the most significant precision engagements in recent weeks, noting that it dealt a major blow to the group’s mobility, logistics and sustainment capability. “The destruction of the motorcycles will significantly reduce the terrorists’ ability to conduct rapid raids and reinforcement across the difficult Lake Chad terrain, while the loss of the food stockpile will further degrade their capacity to sustain prolonged operations,” a senior military source said. It is believed the successful strike is expected to alter the operational dynamics in the Southern Lake Chad axis, particularly around Marte, Ngala and Dikwa Local Government Areas, where ISWAP has continued efforts to regroup and project influence. The Nigerian military has sustained an aggressive air campaign under Operation HADIN KAI, combining precision intelligence, persistent surveillance and targeted strikes against terrorist enclaves to deny insurgents freedom of movement and disrupt their command, logistics and operational networks. The post OPHK drone strikes kill over 20 ISWAP fighters in Lake Chad appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Crisis hits Oyo NDC as members petition national leadership over alleged misconduct
items.description: …Accuse State Chairman of Abuse of Office, Anti-Party Activities By Adeola Badru IBADAN — A crisis is brewing within the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC) in Oyo State as a group of party members has petitioned the national leadership, demanding an investigation into allegations of abuse of office, anti-democratic practices and anti-party conduct against the state chairman, Mrs Oyeronke Akinlolu. The petition, dated July 20, 2026, was addressed to the National Chairman of the party, Senator Cleopas Moses Zuwoghe, through the party’s national secretariat in Abuja. It was signed by Engr. Oshiariyo Stephen Adeyinka and co-signed by other members. Copies of the petition were also sent to the party’s Deputy National Chairman (South West), Alhaji Babatunde Ali, and the National Leader of the party, former Bayelsa State Governor, Senator Henry Seriake Dickson. The aggrieved members said they were acting in the interest of justice, internal democracy, party unity and the long-term growth of the party in Oyo State. In the petition, the members alleged that the actions of the state chairman, if left unchecked, could undermine the credibility, unity and electoral prospects of the party in the state. They accused Akinlolu of allegedly taking unilateral decisions without consulting the State Executive Committee or other relevant organs of the party, contrary to the principles of transparency and collective leadership. The petitioners also alleged that the chairman had weakened internal democracy by excluding members from decision-making processes, discouraging open discussions and creating what they described as an atmosphere of intimidation within the party. They further claimed that more than 50 members were removed from the party’s official Oyo State WhatsApp platform without disciplinary procedures. According to them, the action had affected communication among members and contributed to divisions within the party. The petitioners also accused the state chairman of alleged anti-party conduct, claiming that some of her actions and public statements had raised concerns about her commitment to the ideals and objectives of the NDC. They alleged that recognised executive members had been removed or replaced without following the party’s constitutional procedures or obtaining approval from relevant organs. The members further claimed that the leadership style allegedly adopted by Akinlolu had resulted in factionalisation, mistrust, declining membership and internal disagreements within the Oyo State chapter. The petition also alleged that the state chairman had expressed hostility towards the party’s presidential candidate, Peter Obi, and his running mate, Rabiu Musa Kwankwaso. The petitioners argued that the allegations represented a pattern of leadership inconsistent with the democratic principles, constitutional provisions and vision of the party. They urged the national leadership to constitute an independent panel to investigate the allegations, restore members allegedly removed from official communication platforms pending the outcome of the probe, review executive appointments and replacements allegedly made outside constitutional provisions, and take appropriate disciplinary action if the allegations were proven. The members also called on the party leadership to implement measures aimed at restoring unity, inclusiveness, fairness and confidence among members of the Oyo State chapter. “This petition is made in good faith and in the overall interest of strengthening our great party. We remain committed to the ideals of the Nigeria Democratic Congress and trust that the National Leadership will give this matter the urgent attention it deserves,” the petitioners stated. Mrs Akinlolu and the Oyo State chapter of the party had yet to publicly respond to the allegations as of the time of filing this report. The post Crisis hits Oyo NDC as members petition national leadership over alleged misconduct appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Iran War Live Updates: Trump Threatens Retaliation Against Civilian Targets if Iran Strikes in Strait
Police station opens at Hitler’s birthplace in bid to rid site of Nazi link
items.description: Austria has opened a police station in the house where Adolf Hitler was born in the town of Braunau am Inn. It follows years of controversy about what to do with the 17th-Century former inn, where Nazi dictator Adolf Hitler was born in April 1889. The building has now been substantially refurbished and its facade changed, but it has been a huge headache for Austrian authorities, who want to prevent it from becoming a site of neo-Nazi pilgrimage. Hitler only lived in Salzburger Vorstadt 15 for a few weeks before his family moved to another address in Braunau. They left the town for good when he was three years old. Hitler returned briefly to Braunau in 1938, on his way to Vienna, after he annexed Austria to Nazi Germany. The opening ceremony for the police station, featuring police musicians playing a tuba and other brass instruments, was attended by Interior Minister Gerhard Karner and other local dignitaries. Austria was taking “responsibility” for the “terrible legacy” of the past, said Karner. While deciding what to do with the building had not been easy, Karner believed the police station marked “a new chapter” for the building. “This house will now become a place of democracy, rule of law, security, freedom and human dignity,” he said. A Catholic police chaplain then blessed two crosses which will hang in the new police station. For decades, the interior ministry had rented the building from its former owner, a local woman called Gerlinde Pommer. It paid her a generous rent, in an attempt to prevent the house being used as a site for extreme right-wing tourism. The building was used for many years by a local charity as a day centre and workshop for people with special needs. But it was forced to move out in 2011, when Pommer blocked renovations. She also repeatedly refused to sell the property to the government. The house remained empty for several years, while the authorities and Pommer argued over terms. In 2016, Austria’s parliament passed a law allowing the government to seize the house from Pommer. It paid her compensation of €812,000 (£690,000). She later took the government to court, saying the sum was too low, but lost her case. The interior minister at the time, Wolfgang Sobotka, said the building should be torn down and a new one erected in its place. It was an echo of an earlier offer by a Russian MP who said he wanted to buy the house and blow it up. But there was a storm of criticism, with many saying that destroying the house would be tantamount to denying Austria’s Nazi past. In 2019, the government announced plans to turn it into a police station and initiated an EU-wide competition for architects to submit designs. Millions of euros were spent refurbishing the old yellow building, which now looks very different from before. Now painted white, inside and out, the building is full of gleaming oak woodwork. An old winding staircase and arched entrance hall have been preserved. It looks very different from when the Lebenshilfe charity was running a small shop in one of the rooms at the front, when I last went inside almost 20 years ago. Police Chief Inspector Ludwig Heise said he was happy to have such “a great, modern building” to work in. “We have more space,” he said, “but otherwise it is like any other police station in Austria.” Not everyone is happy with the decision to open a police station there, with some arguing it sent the wrong signal. Some had wanted the house to become a centre of responsibility, confronting the Nazi past. Others, like local historian Florian Kotanko, say they would have liked to have seen the Lebenshilfe charity return. “But even this charity organisation did not want to come back because they thought they would be used as a fig leaf for a political discussion, and so the government had to decide in another way,” Kotanko told the BBC. There is no sign on the house about its history and the building features security cameras to record the activities of any potential neo-Nazi pilgrims. All that links it to Hitler is a stone on the street outside, which was erected in 1989. It says: “Never again Fascism; In memory of millions of dead.” The stone comes from the nearby former concentration camp at Mauthausen. Hitler’s name does not appear. However, Florian Kotanko believes the site’s history will never go away even if fewer neo-Nazis pay a visit. “You have to deal with it, and so we all try to deal with it in an open and correct way. It’s not a matter of being proud, but it’s a matter of dealing with the fact.”

items.title: L’Ouest prépare un grand meeting pour dire merci au président Paul Biya suite à l’élection de Théodore Datouo au perchoir
items.description: © (Ass nat) Élites politiques et autres forces vives de la région se mobilisent pour un rassemblement massif de personnes au stade Omnisports de Toket le samedi 25 juillet prochain. Des populations de la région de l’Ouest en général et celles des Hauts plateaux en particulier se mobilisent pour dire merci au président Paul Biya. La raison en est que leur fils, le député Théodore Datouo est depuis le 17 mars 2026 le président de l’Assemblée nationale. Suite au casting opéré par la hiérarchie du Rassemblement démocratique du peuple camerounais les députés l’ont porté à la tête de la chambre basse du Parlement. Il remplace feu Cavaye Yeguie Djibril. A la suite de cette élection, les populations de la région de l’Ouest sous l’impulsion des cadres du parti au pouvoir issus de la circonscription, veulent dire merci au président national du Rdpc, président de la République. En vue de la circonstance, les élites, en majorité membres du parti au pouvoir ont collecté plus de 110 millions de FCFA. En perspective, l’ambition est de rassembler des centaines de milliers de personnes au stade Omnisports de Toket lors de cet événement. La mobilisation du parti se fait depuis les comités de base jusqu’à la délégation permanente régionale en passant par les sous-sections, sections et délégations permanentes départementales. Les préparatifs englobent tous les aspects événementiels. Le but est de manifester au chef de l’Etat la gratitude de la région qui est l’une des zones du pays où, malgré la montée de l’opposition, les populations continuent de soutenir le parti du flambeau. Et de lui témoigner un ‘’soutien indéfectible’’. L’événement s’inscrit dans une pratique déjà enracinée au Cameroun et consistant à organiser de grands meetings pour dire merci au chef du parti au pouvoir suite à la nomination de personnalités. Ladite pratique se présentant comme une manifestation de réjouissance pour l’intégration des communautés au partage du gâteau national, les localités ne lésinent pas sur les moyens pour l’organiser. Si les meetings servent à mobiliser l’électorat pour les victoires futures au profit du parti au pouvoir, les sommes collectées pour les organiser peuvent servir à améliorer les conditions de vie des populations locales. Cet article L’Ouest prépare un grand meeting pour dire merci au président Paul Biya suite à l’élection de Théodore Datouo au perchoir est apparu en premier sur Journalducameroun.com .

items.title: Islamabad condemns Houthis' threats to Riyadh, warns against targeting Pakistani maritime interests
items.description: Pakistan on Wednesday condemned the Houthis’ recent threats to Saudi Arabia and to trading with the kingdom via the Red Sea, warning that it had the right to self-defence if its “maritime interests” were targeted in “any hostile act”. Islamabad’s statement comes after Yemen’s Houthi rebels , aligned with Iran, announced an immediate maritime embargo on Saudi Arabia, opening a new front in the Middle East conflict. The naval blockade, if enforced, could cut off access to Saudi Arabia’s Red Sea ports, which have been vital for oil exports as renewed US-Iran fighting limits passage through the Strait of Hormuz. “Threats against commercial shipping and trading with the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia are unacceptable and violate established principles of international law,” the Foreign Office (FO) said in a statement on Wednesday, denouncing the “continued threats” by Houthis. Islamabad said it was “particularly concerned by reports of threats directed against vessels engaged in lawful commercial trade” with Saudi Arabia. “Such actions threaten regional security, undermine freedom of navigation, challenge the rules-based maritime order and jeopardise uninterrupted flow of global commerce,” the FO highlighted. Furthermore, it declared that “any hostile act against Pakistani-flagged vessels or Pakistani maritime interests will be regarded as a grave threat to Pakistan’s national security and sovereign interests”. The FO warned that Pakistan reserved its right to “take all necessary measures, including lawful use of force, in the exercise of self-defence to protect its maritime assets and national interests”, consistent with the United Nations Charter and international law. Pakistan also said it was “deeply concerned over attempts to drag” Saudi Arabia into the Middle East conflict, as fighting continues between the United States and Iran, which in turn has targeted American military sites in some Gulf countries. Islamabad reiterated its “unwavering support for the security, sovereignty, territorial integrity and prosperity of the brotherly Kingdom of Saudi Arabia”. “While remaining steadfast in its commitment to dialogue, diplomacy and regional de-escalation, Pakistan calls upon all parties to respect international law and ensure the safety and security of international maritime shipping,” the FO statement affirmed. The FO also shared an Arabic version of the statement on its X account. Houthis’ Monday announcement came after the group and Saudi Arabia traded fire last week for the first time in years, threatening a 2022 truce that has held despite expiring. After Houthi fired missiles at Saudi Arabia on July 13, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif had expressed Pakistan’s strong condemnation of the “blatant attacks” against the kingdom, without further specifying the attacks. Islamabad had also backed Riyadh at an emergency UN Security Council briefing on Yemen, calling for all parties concerned to resolve differences through dialogue and diplomacy. Under a “Strategic Mutual Defence Agreement” signed between the two in September 2025, Pakistan and Saudi Arabia are to treat any attack on either nation as an act of aggression against both. Pakistan and Saudi Arabia have maintained close defence and security ties for decades, and their cooperation has remained an important element of Islamabad’s regional diplomacy. Pakistan had invoked the defence pact through diplomatic intervention in early March to help deter heavier Iranian strikes against Saudi Arabia. Ships change course in Red Sea While it remains unclear how the Houthis would enforce such a blockade, their threats have raised concerns about maritime security and energy shipping in the Red Sea. Four tankers changed their navigational course in the Red Sea on Wednesday, with two signalling the Suez Canal as their new destination, Reuters reports , citing ship tracking data. The vessels all changed their direction, with the other two signalling their destination as open waters in the Red Sea, according to LSEG and MarineTraffic ship tracking data and analysis from British maritime risk management group Vanguard. A fifth ship, a vehicle carrier, appeared to have turned away in the Gulf of Aden on Wednesday after signalling the Saudi port of Jeddah as its next destination, according to shipping data. On Tuesday, three oil tankers loaded with Saudi crude for China and India made U-turns in the Red Sea, heading towards the Suez Canal rather than ‌braving the Yemeni coast at the sea’s mouth, Reuters reported . The Houthis have previously tried to cut off shipping through the Red Sea, leveraging the Bab al-Mandeb strait , a chok­epoint at its southern tip which vessels must navigate when travelling to or from the Suez Canal and the Mediterranean. Saudi Arabia’s Red Sea port of Yanbu has been crucial for exporting oil, bypassing Iran’s effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz during its war with the United States. Crude oil exports from Saudi Arabia fell for a third straight month in May to a record low, data ​from the Joint Organisations Data Initiative (JODI) showed on Tuesday, with the ‌US-Iran conflict disrupting shipments across the Gulf. Exports fell to about 3.434 million barrels per day in May, from 3.986m bpd in April. US President Donald Trump said on Tuesday that his country would “ take care of ” the Houthis if they followed through on the threat to blockade Saudi Arabia’s ports.

items.title: US, Saudi Arabia set to announce civilian nuclear cooperation deal
items.description: The US and Saudi Arabia are expected to announce a deal that will allow the Gulf kingdom to develop a civilian nuclear programme, according to US media reports. An announcement is expected from US President Donald Trump’s administration on Wednesday, US officials were cited as saying. The agreement could be worth billions of dollars to the US, whose companies could be involved in building a facility to produce enriched uranium, which can be used as fuel for power plants but also to make nuclear bombs. Nuclear experts and former officials in Israel – the US’s top ally in the Middle East – have voiced concern that the plan could ultimately lead to Saudi Arabia being able to develop a nuclear weapon. The BBC has asked the White House for comment. The US media reports say that, under the terms of the arrangement, after undertaking a joint study with the US, Saudi Arabia may be allowed to enrich uranium on its soil to produce fuel at a US-built enrichment facility instead of relying on imported fuel. That provision would distinguish this agreement from another one that was struck by the US in 2009 with Saudi Arabia’s neighbour, the United Arab Emirates, under which the UAE does not produce its own fuel and accepts a host of other restrictions. For that reason, the agreement is also expected to attract the concern of nuclear non-proliferation campaigners, whose ultimate aim is to prevent an increase in the number of nuclear weapons worldwide. The nuclear issue has been at the centre of the months-long US-Israeli conflict with Iran. The two nations say one of the reasons they attacked Iran in February was to prevent the country from ever developing a nuclear weapon – something that Tehran denies planning to do. Saudi Arabia’s de facto ruler, Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, told CBS News in 2018 that his own nation was not planning to acquire a nuclear weapon, but that “without a doubt if Iran developed a nuclear bomb, we will follow suit as soon as possible”. The US-Saudi deal due to be unveiled on Wednesday has already been approved by Trump and will last 30 years, according to the Wall Street Journal. If the US objects to enrichment taking place in Saudi Arabia following the joint study, the kingdom would not be allowed to do so on its own or with another foreign partner for 10 years, the newspaper says. The plan will now be sent to the US Congress. Some lawmakers on both sides of the political aisle are also expected to oppose the deal, though Trump’s Republican Party controls both the upper and lower chambers. The US-Saudi deal has not been made contingent on the Saudis normalising diplomatic ties with Israel, the New York Times reports. The push for Saudi-Israeli normalisation has been an aspiration of successive US presidents.

items.title: Affaire Epstein : Daniel Siad, mis en cause par plusieurs plaignantes, retrouvé mort près de Paris
items.description: Daniel Siad, recruteur de mannequins et presente par plusieurs plaignantes comme un intermediaire presume du reseau de Jeffrey Epstein, a ete retrouve mort dans la soiree du 20 juillet a son domicile de Colombes (Hauts-de-Seine). Le parquet de Nanterre a annonce l'ouverture d'une enquete en recherche des causes de la mort, confiee a la police judiciaire des Hauts-de-Seine. Une autopsie doit etre pratiquee afin de determiner les circonstances precises du deces. Selon Le Parisien , l'homme de 69 ans aurait succombe a un arret cardiaque. Au moment de sa mort, il faisait l'objet d'une enquete preliminaire a Paris, menee par l'office specialise dans la lutte contre la traite des etres humains, apres plusieurs plaintes, notamment pour viols. Daniel Siad contestait l'ensemble des accusations portees contre lui. Son avocate, Menya Arab-Tigrine, a affirme qu'il souhaitait etre entendu par les enqueteurs afin de presenter sa version des faits, regrettant qu'aucune audition n'ait eu lieu avant son deces. La premiere plainte avait ete deposee en fevrier par l'ancienne mannequin suedoise Ebba P. Karlsson. Celle-ci affirme avoir identifie Daniel Siad dans les archives declassifiees de l'affaire Epstein et l'accuse de l'avoir violee lorsqu'elle avait 20 ans avant de l'avoir presentee a Gerald Marie, ancien dirigeant de l'agence Elite, egalement mis en cause par plusieurs femmes. Les deux hommes ont toujours nie ces accusations. 📂🇫🇷FLASH INFO - Daniel Siad, soupçonné d’avoir servi de rabatteur au pédocriminel Jeffrey Epstein, a été retrouvé mort à son domicile. Les causes du décès restent à ce jour inconnues. Ça y est, les proches d’Epstein en France vont être mis au ban, et comme ça, pas d’enquête et… — Tribune Populaire🌐 (@TribunePop23) July 22, 2026 Le doute prevaut La mort de Daniel Siad suscite de vives reactions parmi les plaignantes et leurs avocats. Plusieurs estiment que cette disparition prive les victimes potentielles d'une etape importante dans la revelation de la verite. Les conseils d'Ebba P. Karlsson et de Lisa Brinkworth denoncent une « faute grave » du parquet de Paris, estimant que l'interesse aurait dû etre entendu plus rapidement par les enqueteurs. Sur les reseaux sociaux, de nombreux internautes s'interrogent sur les circonstances du deces, certains jugeant cette disparition « troublante » alors que Daniel Siad devait prochainement etre entendu par la justice, selon l'avocate de plusieurs victimes de Jean-Luc Brunel. Cette affaire ravive les comparaisons avec les deces de Jeffrey Epstein, retrouve mort dans sa cellule en 2019, et de Jean-Luc Brunel, qui s'etait suicide en detention en 2022, alors qu'ils faisaient tous deux l'objet de poursuites judiciaires.
