items.title: Wontumi sentence is severe and raises questions of fairness — John Peter Amewu
items.description: Former Minister for Lands and Natural Resources, John Peter Amewu, has described the 20-year prison sentence handed to Ashanti Regional Chairman of the New Patriotic Party (NPP), Bernard Antwi-Boasiako, popularly known as Chairman Wontumi, as “severe” and “shocking”, saying it raises legitimate questions about proportionality, fairness and the administration of justice. In a statement issued on Tuesday, July 22, Mr Amewu expressed solidarity with the convicted politician while emphasising that his position should not be interpreted as opposition to the rule of law or the independence of the judiciary. “I have received with deep shock and profound concern the news of the conviction and custodial sentence imposed on Chairman Bernard Antwi Boasiako,” he said. “As a citizen of Ghana, a former Minister of Lands and Natural Resources, and a longstanding member of the New Patriotic Party tradition, I wish to state clearly that I respect the rule of law, the independence of the judiciary, and the constitutional processes available to every citizen.” Chairman Wontumi was convicted on six counts under Section 99 of the Minerals and Mining (Amendment) Act, 2019, after the Accra High Court found him guilty of unlawfully assigning mineral rights without ministerial approval and facilitating illegal mining in a protected forest reserve through Akonta Mining Limited. He is serving a 20-year prison sentence at the Nsawam Medium Security Prison and has also appeared before a court in connection with a separate illegal mining case involving the Tano Nimiri Forest Reserve. The NPP has indicated that it will pursue all available legal avenues to challenge the conviction and sentence. Mr Amewu, a former NPP Volta Regional Chairman, said he had worked closely with Chairman Wontumi during the party’s rebuilding efforts following its defeat in the 2008 general election. “Chairman Bernard Antwi Boasiako is not just a political figure to me. He is a colleague and a person I have worked closely with in the service of the New Patriotic Party,” he stated. He recalled collaborating with Wontumi and other party executives in mobilising support ahead of the NPP’s return to power in the 2016 general election. While acknowledging the devastating impact of illegal mining, Mr Amewu maintained that the fight against galamsey must be conducted within the bounds of fairness and due process. “The fight against illegal mining must be firm, lawful, and non-partisan,” he said. “At the same time, the fight must be conducted in a manner that preserves justice, proportionality, due process, and public confidence in the legal system.” He argued that the sentence raises broader questions about how Ghana balances environmental protection, criminal accountability, judicial discretion, political neutrality and humane justice. Supports appeal The former Lands Minister welcomed the decision by Chairman Wontumi’s legal team and the NPP to challenge the conviction through the appellate courts. “The appeal courts exist precisely to review questions of law, proportionality, sentencing discretion, and possible errors in principle,” he said. “My solidarity with Chairman Bernard Antwi Boasiako is not a rejection of the rule of law. Rather, it is a call for the rule of law to operate fully, fairly, transparently, and proportionately.” He also urged party supporters to remain calm and allow the legal process to take its course. Mr Amewu extended his support to Chairman Wontumi, his family, the Ashanti Regional leadership of the NPP and the wider party, while calling on Ghanaians to reflect on the country’s justice system. “May justice be done, and may Ghana remain united, lawful, and peaceful,” he concluded.

items.title: Narbona niega haber promocionado a Leire Díez: “Yo no soy la madrina de nadie”
items.description: Leire Díez se ha convertido en una bomba política de efectos retardados que le ha servido al PP de excusa para forzar la comparecencia de Cristina Narbona en el Senado. La presidenta del PSOE ha negado que conociera la presunta trama orquestada por la exmilitante socialista con Santos Cerdán para desestabilizar causas judiciales que afectaban al PSOE, el Gobierno y el entorno familiar de Pedro Sánchez en la comisión de investigación del caso Koldo, un cajón de sastre que la oposición también ha dedicado para arremeter contra José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero y dudar de la legalidad de la financiación del PSOE. Narbona también ha rechazado haber promocionado a Díez y mucho menos haber intermediado en su contratación en las empresas públicas de Enusa y Correos. “Yo no he sido su madrina para nada. Si ha usado mi nombre… Eso pasa, que haya gente que utilice tu nombre para hacerse valer”, ha zanjado la dirigente socialista. Seguir leyendo

items.title: Feijóo promete una “limpieza total” en el Gobierno y espera votar “en más ocasiones” con Vox y Junts en el Congreso
items.description: La mañana siguiente a las elecciones generales del 23 de julio, los periódicos se levantaron con el titular: “El PP gana las elecciones pero necesitará pactar”. Meses después, cuando intentó su investidura con los apoyos insuficientes de Vox, UPN y Coalición Canaria, Alberto Núñez Feijóo acuñó la frase “No soy presidente porque no quiero” tras haber mantenido contactos informales con Junts aquel verano. Luego convocó una decena de manifestaciones contra la amnistía pactada por Pedro Sánchez con los nacionalistas catalanes. Hoy, transcurridos tres años, aprovechando el tercer aniversario de las urnas del 23-J y en el cierre de un curso político en el que ha endurecido su ofensiva por los casos de corrupción que atenazan al PSOE , Feijóo ha hecho balance de una legislatura que da por muerta. “Sánchez no merece otra cosa que el mayor castigo electoral porque ha sido una desgracia para este país. España ya ha cambiado de opinión”, ha sentenciado Feijóo, este miércoles, en una comparecencia en la que se ha ofrecido como la alternativa frente a la corrupción “sistémica” del Ejecutivo. “No será un relevo, será una limpieza total”, ha prometido. items.title: ADC crisis ends in Kaduna as Ashiru reconciles factional leaders
items.description: By Ibrahim Hassan-Wuyo The governorship candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) in Kaduna State, Hon. Isa Ashiru, has brokered peace among the party’s factional leaders, bringing an end to the leadership crisis that had divided the state chapter. The reconciliation meeting, which lasted about three hours at Ashiru’s office in Kaduna, brought together factional chairmen and other key stakeholders of the party in the state. Those in attendance included Elder Patrick Ambut, Ben Bako, Tijjani Mustapha, Bege Katuka, the party’s Zonal Vice Chairman, Hajiya Hafsat Baba, and other stakeholders. Speaking after the meeting, Ambut commended Ashiru’s leadership and expressed confidence in his ability to reposition the party ahead of the 2027 general elections. He described the meeting as a turning point for the Kaduna chapter, saying all factions had agreed to work together in the interest of the party. “The success of the ADC in the forthcoming general elections is our priority, and the issue of factionalisation in Kaduna has now been laid to rest,” Ambut said. Also speaking, Tijjani Mustapha described the meeting, which was attended by 11 party leaders, as cordial and productive, adding that a communiqué detailing the resolutions reached would be issued soon. Ben Bako said the leadership crisis within the Kaduna ADC had been resolved, noting that efforts were ongoing to address pending legal matters involving the party. “Leadership is the ability to unite people and guide them towards a common goal, and that is exactly what has happened here today,” Bako said. The reconciliation is expected to strengthen internal cohesion within the ADC and improve the party’s preparedness for future political engagements in Kaduna State. The post ADC crisis ends in Kaduna as Ashiru reconciles factional leaders appeared first on Vanguard News .

International Criminal Court's members to vote on firing prosecutor Khan over alleged misconduct
items.description: Member states of the International Criminal Court (ICC) will vote on Friday on whether to fire chief prosecutor Karim Khan for alleged sexual misconduct in a case ‌that has damaged the tribunal’s credibility at a time when the US has ramped up its attacks on the court. Khan, 56, denies any wrongdoing. The vote on his fate comes just a week after Washington announced a renewed diplomatic campaign to dismantle the ICC, which it says is a threat to US sovereignty. “The court is already in a precarious state,” Mark Ellis, the executive director of the International Bar Association and a longtime ​ICC observer, told Reuters . “The Khan situation is a separate but very significant part of the overall perception of the court’s credibility,” he added. The US has said no diplomatic ​options are off the table in its campaign against the ICC, which has already seen 11 of the ICC’s top officials facing sanctions. Accuser points to power disparity, Khan denies wrongdoing The Khan vote also lays bare internal divisions among the court’s governing bodies and its member states. While officials from the court’s governing body said Khan should ​be dismissed because he committed serious misconduct, according to documents seen by Reuters , it remains unclear if member states will follow that recommendation. For him to be removed, an absolute majority of ​at least 63 of the court’s 125 member states will have to agree in the secret ballot to be held at a meeting at the UN headquarters in New York. The vote is due around 3 pm (1900 GMT). Khan’s accuser, a junior ICC staffer, spoke publicly for the first time last week in an interview with CNN in which she repeated her allegations that the prosecutor had a non-consensual sexual relationship with her. “There ​is no way for something to be consensual when you have such a power disparity,” the woman, identified only by her first name Sarah, told CNN . Lawyers for Khan told Reuters ​he denied “any sexual relationship of any kind” with the alleged victim. They called the decision that led to Friday’s vote unlawful, procedurally unfair and unsupported by evidence, and pointed to a panel of independent judges ‌who found there ⁠was no evidence to establish the allegations beyond reasonable doubt after a report by UN investigators into the matter. Vote will not end ICC’s internal turmoil Whatever the outcome, the vote will not put an end to internal divisions. If ICC member states dismiss Khan, it will be seen by some as the result of political pressure after Khan sought arrest warrants for senior Israeli officials for alleged war crimes committed in Gaza. Israel is not a member of the court and has dismissed the warrants as bogus. It would also set off an election process for a new prosecutor, ​which is not expected to finish before the ​start of next year. If Khan survives the ⁠vote, his position would be weakened and it would give critics of the court another stick to beat it with. The ICC was established in 2002 by the international community to prosecute war crimes, genocide and crimes against humanity. It asserts jurisdiction only if one of its ​125 member states is unable or unwilling to prosecute atrocities itself. The United States has never been a member of the court. However, ​the ICC statute also gives ⁠the court the power to prosecute atrocity crimes committed on the territory of member states by nationals of non-member states. A State Department official, speaking on condition of anonymity, said last week Washington was considering diplomatic pressure on other nations to withdraw from the court. The EU has reiterated its support for the ICC and said threats against the court or its elected officials are not acceptable. ⁠However, the EU ​and most ICC member states have yet to take concrete public steps that could shield the institution. The International ​Federation for Human Rights, a human rights NGO working with victims in cases being considered by the ICC, called the new US campaign the most serious attack to date on the world’s only permanent international criminal court. “States created ​the ICC together; they now have the opportunity — and the responsibility — to defend it together,” Secretary General Drissa Traore said.

items.title: 9 benefits immigrants enjoy after becoming US citizens
items.description: Naturalised US citizens gain significant rights, protections, and privileges. While lawful permanent residents (Green Card holders) enjoy many benefits, full citizenship unlocks legal security and civic participation that are otherwise unavailable. Here are 9 key benefits immigrants enjoy after becoming US citizens: 1. Protection Against Deportation As a US citizen, your right to live in the United States is permanent. Lawful permanent residents can still face deportation proceedings if they are convicted of certain crimes or remain outside the country for extended periods. Citizenship permanently removes these risks. 2. The Right to Vote Citizenship grants full voting rights in federal, state, and local elections. Voting allows naturalised citizens to choose political leaders, influence policy decisions, and participate directly in American democracy. 3. Ability to Run for Public Office Naturalised citizens can run for most elected public offices in the United States, including local school boards, city councils, state legislatures, and the U.S. Congress (House of Representatives and Senate). The only position reserved exclusively for natural-born citizens is the US Presidency. 4. Broad Family Sponsoring Privileges While Green Card holders can sponsor spouses and unmarried children, US citizens can petition for a broader group of family members—including parents, siblings, and adult married children. Additionally, immediate relatives (spouses, minor children, and parents) are not subject to annual visa quotas, significantly speeding up the immigration process. 5. Automatic Citizenship for Unmarried Children Under US immigration law, minor children (under 18 years old) who are lawful permanent residents automatically derive US citizenship when their parent naturalises, without needing to go through a separate naturalisation application process. 6. Unlimited Freedom to Travel and Live Abroad Citizens can travel outside the US for as long as they wish without risking their legal status. Green Card holders must maintain continuous residence in the US and can lose their status if they stay abroad too long without special re-entry permits. US citizens also gain access to one of the world’s most travel-friendly passports. 7. Access to Restricted Federal Jobs Many federal government jobs—including positions in foreign service, intelligence agencies (such as the CIA and FBI), federal law enforcement, and roles requiring security clearances—are legally reserved for US citizens. 8. Eligibility for Federal Grants and Financial Assistance Citizens are eligible for all federal public assistance programs, government grants, and federal college financial aid (including specific government-backed scholarships and fellowships) without facing the restrictions or “public charge” scrutiny that non-citizens might encounter. 9. Freedom from Renewal Fees and Paperwork Maintaining permanent residency requires renewing your Green Card every 10 years (Form I-90), filing name/address changes promptly, and paying recurring government fees. Naturalisation eliminates these ongoing costs, paperwork, and administrative maintenance for life. The post 9 benefits immigrants enjoy after becoming US citizens appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Maroc : un rappeur installé en France détenu pour avoir chanté “Pegasus”
items.description: Quelques jours après Ali Lmrabet , arrêté à l’aéroport de Tanger à son retour d’Espagne, la presse étrangère fait état de l’arrestation au Maroc d’une autre voix critique à l'égard de la monarchie. Pour les Marocains qui expriment librement leurs opinions à partir de l’étranger, il est décidément périlleux de rentrer dans leur pays. Selon le journaliste espagnol Ignacio Cembrero, le rappeur marocain Mehdi Lyoubi, connu sous son nom de scène de Mehdi Black Wind, est actuellement en détention provisoire à la prison d'Oukacha, à Casablanca. Mehdi Lyoubi chante une chanson intitulée “Pegasus” et se retrouve en prison au Maroc Résidant en France, le rappeur s'était rendu au Maroc pour rendre visite à sa famille. Dès son arrivée, il a été interpellé. Selon Cembrero, il est reproché au chanteur “les paroles de ses chansons critiquant la monarchie”. Moroccan rapper Mehdi Lyoubi, better known by his stage name Mehdi Black Wind, is being held in pretrial detention at Oukacha Prison in Casablanca over the lyrics of his songs criticizing the monarchy. One of them is titled #Pegasus . Living in France, he had traveled to #Morocco … — Ignacio Cembrero (@icembrero) July 21, 2026 L'une des chansons de Mehdi Black Wind s'intitule “Pegasus”, du nom du logiciel espion israélien utilisé par les services marocains depuis 2017 pour écouter les téléphones de journalistes et d’opposants, mais aussi de dirigeants étrangers. Éclaté en 2021, le scandale est revenu ces derniers jours au-devant de la scène avec de nouvelles révélations et preuves qui incriminent gravement le Maroc. Le rappeur se serait rendu au Maroc pour les besoins d’un film, indique pour sa part le journaliste français Edwy Plenel. “El Mahdi Lyoubi, rappeur marocain connu sous le nom de scène de Mehdi Black Wind et résident français, a été arrêté au Maroc alors qu’il effectuait des repérages pour un film. Emprisonné, il est poursuivi pour ses textes critiques envers la monarchie”, a écrit sur X le fondateur de Médiapart. El Mahdi Lyoubi, rappeur marocain connu sous le nom de scène de Mehdi Black Wind et résident français, a été arrêté au Maroc alors qu’il effectuait des repérages pour un film. Emprisonné, il est poursuivi pour ses textes critiques envers la monarchie. https://t.co/3pqHofyIhP — Edwy Plenel (@edwyplenel) July 21, 2026 Ali Lmrabet raconte sa “séquestration illégale” Samedi 11 juillet, le journaliste et opposant Ali Lmrabet, installé depuis plusieurs années en Espagne, a été arrêté à l’aéroport de Tanger. Remis en liberté, il a dénoncé sur X les conditions rocambolesques de son interpellation et les humiliations qu’il a subies lors de son transfert de Tanger à Casablanca. “J’ai été remis quasi secrètement, dans un lieu caché et éloigné de l’entrée de l’aéroport, à 7 brutes armées non identifiées. Elles m’ont brutalisé avant de me jeter dans une fourgonnette sans aucun distinctif, dépourvue de sièges et de plaque d’immatriculation. J’ai effectué les 350 kilomètres séparant Tanger de Casablanca dans des conditions indignes. Pendant tout le trajet, j’ai été physiquement molesté et je n’ai pas été autorisé à utiliser des toilettes”, a raconté le journaliste, promettant de revenir plus en détail “sur cette séquestration illégale et sur les conditions dans lesquelles elle s’est déroulée”. 7. J’ai ensuite été remis quasi secrètement, dans un lieu caché et éloigné de l’entrée de l’aéroport, à 7 brutes armées non identifiées. Elles m’ont brutalisé avant de me jeter dans une fourgonnette sans aucun distinctif, dépourvue de sièges et de plaque d’immatriculation. — Ali Lmrabet علي المرابط (@Alilmrabet) July 20, 2026 L’article Maroc : un rappeur installé en France détenu pour avoir chanté “Pegasus” est apparu en premier sur TSA .

items.title: Budget 2027, santé, universités : le gouvernement prépare ses grands chantiers de rentrée
items.description: À quelques semaines de la reprise de l'activité politique et parlementaire, l'exécutif accélère sur plusieurs dossiers stratégiques. Réuni mercredi à Rabat sous la présidence du Chef du gouvernement Aziz Akhannouch, le Conseil de gouvernement se penchera sur les grandes orientations budgétaires pour 2027, la transformation du système de santé, la gouvernance des universités ainsi que plusieurs mesures économiques et sociales. À l'approche de la rentrée politique, le gouvernement souhaite afficher ses priorités et préparer les prochaines étapes de son action. L'ordre du jour du Conseil illustre cette volonté d'avancer simultanément sur plusieurs chantiers : consolidation des équilibres économiques, poursuite des réformes sociales et modernisation des services publics. Le premier dossier d'importance porte sur la préparation du projet de loi de finances 2027. La ministre de l'Économie et des Finances présentera un exposé sur l'état d'exécution de la loi de finances 2026, l'avancement des travaux préparatoires du prochain budget ainsi que la programmation budgétaire triennale couvrant la période 2027-2029. Ce rendez-vous marque une étape clé dans l'élaboration des orientations budgétaires des prochaines années. Les arbitrages devront concilier plusieurs impératifs : maintenir un niveau élevé d'investissement public, financer les réformes sociales, soutenir les secteurs productifs tout en préservant les grands équilibres des finances publiques. La santé numérique au cœur de la réforme Le Conseil examinera également un projet de loi relatif au système national intégré d'information sanitaire, présenté comme l'un des piliers de la réforme du secteur de la santé. Le texte vise à fluidifier la circulation des données médicales, renforcer la coordination entre les différents acteurs du système sanitaire et moderniser les outils de pilotage. La digitalisation devrait notamment améliorer le suivi des patients, la qualité des soins et l'efficacité de la gestion hospitalière. Cette initiative s'inscrit dans la refonte plus large du système de santé engagée par le Royaume afin d'améliorer l'accès aux soins et d'accompagner la généralisation de la protection sociale. Plusieurs textes réglementaires concernant le secteur pharmaceutique seront également examinés. Ils portent notamment sur l'application de certaines dispositions du Code du médicament et de la pharmacie ainsi que sur l'évolution des règles de fixation des prix des médicaments fabriqués localement ou importés. Gouvernance universitaire : de nouvelles règles en préparation L'enseignement supérieur figure lui aussi parmi les principaux dossiers de cette réunion. Plusieurs projets de décret concernent la gouvernance des établissements universitaires. Ils portent notamment sur les conditions de nomination de certains responsables administratifs, les modalités d'élection des chefs de département ainsi que le régime indemnitaire applicable aux responsables universitaires. À travers ces mesures, l'exécutif poursuit son objectif de moderniser le fonctionnement des universités et de renforcer leur efficacité administrative. L'ambition est de mieux préparer les établissements aux défis liés à la qualité de la formation, au développement de la recherche scientifique et à l'amélioration de l’employabilité des diplômés. Cette évolution s'inscrit dans une réforme plus large de l'enseignement supérieur destinée à rapprocher davantage l'université des besoins économiques et sociaux du pays. Pouvoir d'achat et approvisionnement : des mesures de soutien Le Conseil examinera également plusieurs textes à caractère économique et social. Parmi eux figure un projet de décret prévoyant la prolongation du droit d'importation appliqué au blé tendre et à ses dérivés. Un autre texte prévoit la suspension des droits d'importation sur les ovins vivants ainsi que sur certaines viandes bovines, ovines, caprines et camélines. Ces mesures s'inscrivent dans la stratégie des pouvoirs publics visant à sécuriser l'approvisionnement du marché national et à limiter les tensions sur certains produits sensibles, dans un contexte toujours marqué par les contraintes climatiques et la volatilité des marchés internationaux. Le gouvernement examinera également des textes relatifs au Pass Jeunes, au Prix du Maroc pour la jeunesse ainsi qu'à la réforme des Centres régionaux d'investissement. Ce dernier chantier vise à améliorer l'accompagnement des investisseurs et à renforcer l'attractivité économique des territoires. Coopération internationale et nominations Les travaux du Conseil porteront également sur plusieurs dossiers institutionnels, notamment l'organisation administrative des établissements pénitentiaires ainsi que l'application de certaines dispositions relatives au régime d'aide sociale directe. Sur le plan international, l'exécutif examinera plusieurs accords de coopération, notamment avec le Burkina Faso dans le domaine de la protection civile, ainsi que des textes liés à l'adhésion du Maroc à différents mécanismes africains de coopération. La réunion devrait enfin s'achever par l'examen de propositions de nomination à de hautes fonctions, conformément aux dispositions de la Constitution. Une réunion charnière avant la rentrée Au-delà de la diversité des sujets inscrits à l'ordre du jour, cette réunion du Conseil de gouvernement constitue un rendez-vous important avant la rentrée politique. Les prochains mois seront marqués par les débats autour du budget 2027, la poursuite des réformes sociales et les choix économiques appelés à accompagner les transformations engagées par le Royaume. Entre la préparation du prochain budget, la réforme du système de santé, la modernisation de l'enseignement supérieur et les mesures destinées à soutenir le pouvoir d'achat, l'exécutif cherche à donner un nouvel élan à son action. Les décisions qui seront adoptées à l'issue du Conseil permettront de préciser les priorités qui guideront l'action gouvernementale au cours des prochains mois. The post Budget 2027, santé, universités : le gouvernement prépare ses grands chantiers de rentrée appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: Father dies while attempting to rescue daughter from well in Kano
items.description: By Hadiza Yusuf A 45-year-old man, Abubakar Abdullahi, has died while attempting to rescue his 15-year-old daughter, Fiddausi Abubakar, who accidentally fell into an old dry well in Yancibi Village, Tsanyawa Local Government Area of Kano State. The Kano State Fire Service confirmed the incident in a statement issued on Wednesday by its Public Relations Officer, ACFO Saminu Yusif Abdullahi. According to the statement, the Tsanyawa Fire Service Branch received a distress call on Monday evening, July 21, reporting that a father and his daughter had fallen into a well. Firefighters were immediately deployed to the scene and commenced rescue operations. However, the service said the operation was suspended that night due to darkness and the challenging conditions surrounding the well. It added that an emergency rescue team was later dispatched from the Fire Service headquarters in the early hours of Tuesday to assist in the operation. “Upon arrival, the team successfully recovered both victims from the well. They were rescued unconscious and were later confirmed dead,” the statement said. The Fire Service said the bodies were subsequently handed over to the Village Head of Yancibi, Shamsu Sani. Preliminary findings indicated that the teenage girl accidentally fell into the abandoned well, while her father entered in an attempt to rescue her but became trapped, resulting in the death of both victims. The Kano State Fire Service expressed condolences to the family of the deceased and advised residents against entering wells or similar confined spaces without proper safety equipment. It also urged members of the public to immediately contact emergency response agencies whenever such incidents occur to prevent further loss of lives. The post Father dies while attempting to rescue daughter from well in Kano appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: ‘War is over’ — Fubara declares support for Tinubu, Wike’s Rainbow Coalition
items.description: Rivers State Governor, Siminalayi Fubara, has declared his support for President Bola Tinubu and the Rainbow Coalition, a political movement led by the Minister of the Federal Capital Territory, Nyesom Wike, ahead of the 2027 general elections. Fubara made the declaration on Tuesday during the groundbreaking ceremony for the reconstruction and equipping of the Riven Medical Facility, a public-private sector pharmaceutical factory in Rivers State. The governor said the prolonged political crisis in the state had come to an end, urging his supporters to unite behind all candidates presented by the coalition. “I am supporting our Rainbow Coalition. President Bola Ahmed Tinubu is our President, and he is the one we are supporting. The governorship candidate is the candidate I am supporting, and anybody who believes in me will support every candidate under the Rainbow Coalition,” Fubara said. He dismissed suggestions that he should delay aligning with the coalition, insisting that he had already made his choice. “I am already in the family. I have returned to our family. Give your total support to the coalition. The time of war is over; it is now time for peace, and that peace will take us to another level,” he added. The governor also ruled out any involvement in activities capable of reigniting political divisions in the state. “I am not party to anything that will bring any form of disunity in this state. We have done what we need to do, and now we are moving forward,” he said. Speaking on the Riven Medical Facility project, Fubara reaffirmed his administration’s commitment to its successful execution, saying he agreed to support the investment after receiving assurances on its viability and delivery timeline. He disclosed that the first phase of the pharmaceutical factory is expected to be completed within three months, describing it as a strategic investment that would promote industrialisation, create employment and position Rivers State as a hub for the production of medical consumables. According to the governor, the project is expected to stimulate economic activities, attract local and international patronage, and encourage the establishment of complementary industries around the facility. Fubara pledged the state government’s full support for the investors and urged members of the host community to cooperate with the company to ensure the project’s success. He also revealed that the state government is currently involved in litigation with several companies over claims exceeding ₦200 billion, stressing that his administration is determined to avoid contractual disputes that could discourage investors. The governor expressed optimism that the first phase of the project would be ready for commissioning within the next three months. The post ‘War is over’ — Fubara declares support for Tinubu, Wike’s Rainbow Coalition appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: 2026 Mid-Year Budget Review: Traders hope for lower prices and relief from high cost of living
items.description: Some Ghanaians in Accra’s Central Business District have expressed mixed expectations ahead of the presentation of the 2026 Mid-Year Budget Review, with some saying they are beginning to experience signs of economic improvement while others insist the gains have yet to translate into lower prices and improved livelihoods. Speaking to JoyNews ‘ Michelle Lartey ahead of Finance Minister Dr Cassiel Ato Forson’s budget presentation on Thursday, July 23, some traders said they were looking to the government to introduce measures that would ease the cost of doing business and reduce the prices of goods. Some traders acknowledged that recent economic indicators suggest the economy is stabilising and urged the government to sustain the reforms that have contributed to the progress. Others, however, said they were yet to feel any meaningful change in their day-to-day activities, arguing that the high cost of living continues to put pressure on businesses and households. The traders also called on the government to simplify the presentation of economic policies and budget figures to enable ordinary Ghanaians to better understand how the measures affect their lives. One trader said her biggest expectation is a reduction in market prices following the budget announcement. “I’m hoping that the prices of goods in the market will reduce after the budget is read. Things should become a bit more affordable for us because prices are still increasing,” she said. Another shopper said, “Some people are raising prices on their own, so if the government can do its best to ensure there’s some reduction in the market, it will be okay for us.” Another added, “I want the government to do better because if I come to the market with GH¢1,000, I want to be able to buy many of the things I need. Right now, the items I’m carrying cost almost GH¢1,200,” she lamented. The views reflect the broader expectations of many Ghanaians, who will be watching closely to see whether the Mid-Year Budget Review outlines measures capable of easing the cost of living while sustaining the country’s recent economic recovery.

items.title: Senate urges FG to audit licensed miners, establish compensation scheme for mining workers
items.description: By Henry Umoru ABUJA — The Senate has urged the Federal Government, through the Ministry of Solid Minerals Development, to conduct an audit of licensed miners and their areas of operation across the country to strengthen monitoring and regulation of mining activities. The upper chamber also called for the establishment of a statutory Mining Workers’ Compensation Scheme, to be funded through mandatory contributions by licensed mining operators, to provide compensation for occupational injuries, permanent disabilities, occupational diseases and deaths arising from mining activities. The resolutions were reached on Wednesday following the consideration of a motion titled: “Urgent Need to Address the Escalating Safety Risks, Environmental Hazards and Humanitarian Concerns Arising from Unsafe Coal Mining Activities in Ankpa and Other Coal-Bearing Communities in Kogi State.” The motion was sponsored by Senator Isah Jibrin (APC, Kogi East) under matters of urgent national importance. The Senate also urged the National Insurance Commission (NAICOM), in collaboration with the Ministry of Solid Minerals Development, to develop compulsory occupational accident insurance for all categories of miners, including artisanal and small-scale miners operating under recognised mining cooperatives or community mining arrangements. It further called for the establishment of a Mine Rehabilitation and Victims Support Fund to provide emergency medical treatment, rehabilitation and compensation for injured miners, educational support for children of deceased miners, and livelihood assistance for affected families. The Senate mandated its Committees on Solid Minerals Development; Environment; Labour, Employment and Productivity; and Health to engage relevant ministries, departments and agencies on the implementation of the resolutions and report back within six weeks. The chamber also observed a minute silence in honour of victims of the landslide that occurred at Alufele in Enjema District, within the Ankpa coal mining area of Kogi State, on July 14, 2026. Presenting the motion, Senator Jibrin said coal mining remained a major economic activity in Ankpa Local Government Area, particularly in coal-bearing communities such as Enjema, Okaba and Ikah, as well as parts of Omala and Olamaboro Local Government Areas within the Ankpa Coal Block of the Anambra Basin. He noted that the coal deposits in the area are overlain by lateritic materials, loose sandstone, mudstone, clay, shale and unconsolidated sediments, which make the terrain vulnerable to subsidence, landslides and ground failure, especially under heavy mechanical pressure. The lawmaker expressed sadness over the July 14, 2026 incident in Alufele, where a ground failure trapped artisanal and small-scale miners underground, while several trucks reportedly sank into mining pits during loading operations, resulting in the death of 20 workers and injuries to others. He said the incident exposed the dangerous conditions under which mining activities were being carried out in the area. Jibrin raised concerns over unsafe mining practices, particularly the use of room-and-pillar mining methods without adequate reinforcement using timber, steel or concrete supports, which he said had increased the risks of mine collapses and fatal accidents. The senator also expressed concern that the location of heavy-duty loading points close to unstable mining pits had worsened geological risks, exposing miners, transport operators and nearby communities to avoidable dangers. He further noted that many artisanal miners operate without employment contracts, social security protection, health insurance, life insurance or occupational hazard insurance, leaving victims and their families without financial support after accidents. According to him, families of deceased miners and those who suffer permanent injuries are often left without adequate compensation, rehabilitation or alternative sources of livelihood despite the economic benefits derived from mineral exploitation. The Senate expressed concern that repeated mine collapses, landslides and related accidents posed serious threats to human lives, the environment, economic activities and public safety. It stressed the need for urgent legislative and executive action to introduce mandatory insurance coverage and enforceable compensation mechanisms for artisanal and small-scale miners. The Senate noted that leading mining jurisdictions had institutionalised compulsory workers’ compensation insurance and occupational health protection schemes, under which mining operators are required to provide medical care, rehabilitation, disability benefits and compensation to affected workers and families. It warned that without immediate intervention, unsafe mining practices and unstable geological conditions in the Ankpa Coal Block could lead to recurring mine disasters, environmental degradation and loss of valuable mining assets. The post Senate urges FG to audit licensed miners, establish compensation scheme for mining workers appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: 3e édition du Camp vacances musique : L’Association des artistes de la Police nationale donne de la joie aux enfants de Fds et de Vdp
items.description: La 3e édition du Camp vacances musique de l’Association des artistes de la Police nationale (2APN) a pris fin le mercredi 15 juillet 2026 à Pô. L’objectif de ce camp est d’initier les enfants des Fds et de Vdp aux instruments de musique et au civisme. Une centaine d’enfants ont pris part à ce camp vacances musique qui a duré deux semaines dans le Nahouri. Le président de l’association russo-burkinabè « African Initiative », Ayo Soumaïla Azenwo, était l’un des parrains de cette 3e édition du camp vacances musique. « À vous, chers campeurs, je voudrais vous dire ceci : n’oubliez jamais de mettre en pratique ce que vous avez appris ici. Continuez à chanter, à partager, à croire en vous, car le Burkina Faso aura besoin de votre intelligence », a lancé Nestor Ollo Nouffé, secrétaire général de l’Association des artistes de la Police nationale. Nouffé Ollo Nestor, secrétaire général de l’Association des artistes de la Police nationale, a exhorté les campeurs à partager les connaissances acquises lors de ce camp avec leurs amis. Pendant 14 jours, des enfants de Fds et de Vdp se sont familiarisés avec les instruments de musique. Ils ont également appris les bases de la musique grâce à l’encadrement des instructeurs. « L’objectif était de donner de la joie aux enfants tout en leur inculquant l’amour de la musique. En deux semaines, nous avons pu inculquer beaucoup de notions aux enfants », a laissé entendre Wahabou Bébané, président de l’Association des artistes de la Police nationale. Wahou Bébané s’est dit satisfait du niveau d’apprentissage des campeurs. Plusieurs activités ont également rythmé le camp de Pô. Les campeurs ont pu découvrir la ville de Pô et la culture kasséna. « Nous avons profité apprendre la langue kasséna, les pas de danses kasséna et tout ce qui est de la culture kasséna. Les campeurs, ont également visité le palais royal de Tiébélé », a indiqué Whabou Bébané. Les campeurs ont également appris à confectionner des objets utilitaires pendant ce camp vacances musique. Ils ont profité de la cérémonie de clôture pour présenter leurs chefs-d’œuvre au public. « Nous repartons avec des souvenirs qui resteront dans nos cœurs. Nous remercions les encadreurs pour leur patience, les organisateurs pour tous les sacrifices consentis ainsi que les partenaires qui ont rendu cette belle aventure possible », a déclaré la représentante des campeurs. L’Association russo-burkinabè « African Initiative », a soutenu ce camp vacances musique. Ayo Soumaïla Azenwo, président d’African Initiative et co-parrain de cette 3e édition, a félicité les initiateurs de ce Camp vacances musique et réitéré son soutien pour les prochaines éditions. Ayo Soumaïla Azenwo, président d’African Initiative a félicité les campeurs pour leur volonté manifeste d’apprendre. Les campeurs ont aussi bénéficié d’une projection de film et d’un cours de langue Russe offert par « African Initiative ». C’est la deuxième fois qu’African Initiative soutient le Camp vacances musique de l’Association des artistes de la Police nationale L’article 3e édition du Camp vacances musique : L’Association des artistes de la Police nationale donne de la joie aux enfants de Fds et de Vdp est apparu en premier sur Faso7 .

items.title: Project analysts and consultants: standard-bearers for excellence in business project management
items.description: In an era defined by rapid technological change, economic uncertainty, and increasing stakeholder expectations, the success of organizations is no longer determined solely by vision or ambition. Success is increasingly measured by the ability to transform ideas into tangible results through well-managed projects. Across both the public and private sectors, projects have become the primary vehicles through which strategic objectives are achieved, infrastructure is developed, innovation is delivered, and sustainable growth is realized. At the center of this transformation stand project analysts and consultants—professionals whose expertise, discipline, and commitment to excellence make them indispensable contributors to organizational success. As standard-bearers for excellence in business project management, they play a vital role in ensuring that projects are properly conceived, strategically aligned, efficiently executed, and successfully completed. The growing complexity of today’s business environment has elevated the importance of professional project management. Organizations face increasing pressure to deliver projects within scope, on schedule, within budget, and in compliance with quality standards. Yet many projects continue to encounter challenges ranging from inadequate planning and poor stakeholder engagement to ineffective risk management and weak governance structures. These shortcomings often result in delays, cost overruns, and unrealized benefits. Project analysts and consultants help organizations navigate these challenges by providing the expertise required to assess project feasibility, analyze business needs, identify risks, develop implementation strategies, and establish performance measurement frameworks. Their work extends beyond technical project administration; it encompasses strategic thinking, business analysis, change management, and organizational improvement. A competent project analyst serves as the bridge between organizational objectives and project execution. Through data-driven analysis and informed decision-making, project analysts help organizations identify opportunities, define requirements, and optimize resource allocation. Their ability to interpret complex information and translate it into actionable insights enables decision-makers to make informed choices that contribute to project success. Similarly, project consultants bring specialized knowledge and independent perspectives that strengthen organizational capacity. By advising on project methodologies, governance frameworks, risk management practices, and performance improvement initiatives, consultants help organizations adopt best practices that enhance efficiency and effectiveness. Their contributions are particularly valuable in large-scale and high-impact projects where strategic oversight and expert guidance are critical. The importance of these professionals is especially evident in national development initiatives. Governments around the world rely on effective project management to deliver infrastructure, healthcare systems, educational reforms, energy projects, and digital transformation programs. The quality of project planning and execution directly influences economic growth, social development, and public confidence. Where projects are professionally managed, communities benefit from timely delivery, improved services, and greater value for public investment. In the corporate sector, project analysts and consultants are equally essential. Businesses operate in highly competitive environments where innovation, agility, and operational excellence determine long-term sustainability. Whether launching new products, implementing technology solutions, expanding into new markets, or undertaking organizational transformation, businesses depend on project management professionals to ensure successful outcomes. Their ability to align projects with business objectives contributes significantly to profitability, customer satisfaction, and competitive advantage. Recognizing the growing significance of these professionals, the Chartered Institute of Project Analysts and Consultants continues to play a pivotal role in advancing the standards and practice of business project management. As a professional body mandated to advance business project management, the Institute serves as a catalyst for professional excellence, ethical conduct, capacity development, and industry advancement. Through professional certification, continuing education, research, advocacy, and stakeholder engagement, the Institute promotes the development of highly competent project analysts and consultants capable of meeting contemporary organizational challenges. By establishing professional standards and encouraging adherence to globally recognized best practices, the Institute contributes to strengthening project management capabilities across industries and sectors. Professional development remains a cornerstone of effective project management. As technologies evolve and organizational needs become more sophisticated, practitioners must continually enhance their knowledge and skills. Emerging trends such as artificial intelligence, data analytics, sustainability management, digital transformation, and agile methodologies are reshaping the project management landscape. Professionals who remain committed to lifelong learning are better positioned to deliver value and drive innovation. The Chartered Institute of Project Analysts and Consultants recognizes this reality and continues to invest in initiatives that promote professional growth and knowledge exchange. Through conferences, workshops, seminars, publications, and collaborative partnerships, the Institute creates opportunities for practitioners to remain informed about industry developments and emerging best practices. These efforts not only enhance individual competencies but also contribute to raising the overall quality of project management practice. Equally important is the promotion of ethics and professionalism. Effective project management requires more than technical expertise; it demands integrity, accountability, transparency, and responsible leadership. Stakeholders place significant trust in project professionals to manage resources, oversee critical initiatives, and make decisions that affect organizations and communities. Upholding ethical standards is therefore fundamental to maintaining public confidence and professional credibility. As organizations increasingly recognize the strategic importance of projects, the demand for qualified project analysts and consultants is expected to continue growing. This presents significant opportunities for aspiring professionals seeking meaningful careers that contribute to organizational success and societal development. It also underscores the need for robust professional institutions capable of supporting competence development and maintaining standards of excellence. The future of business project management will be shaped by professionals who can combine analytical rigor with strategic vision, technical competence with ethical leadership, and innovation with disciplined execution. Project analysts and consultants are uniquely positioned to fulfill this role. Their contributions extend beyond individual projects to influence organizational performance, economic development, and societal progress. As standard-bearers for excellence in business project management, project analysts and consultants represent a profession whose value continues to grow in an increasingly project-driven world. Through their dedication to excellence and the leadership provided by the Chartered Institute of Project Analysts and Consultants, the profession is helping to build stronger organizations, deliver better outcomes, and create sustainable value for stakeholders across all sectors. The message is clear: excellence in business project management is not accidental. It is the product of professional competence, ethical practice, continuous learning, and institutional support. By championing these principles, project analysts and consultants will continue to lead the way in transforming ideas into achievements and aspirations into measurable results. Amo Agyapong is a seasoned Organisational Development Consultant, Competency & Occupational Standards Consultant, and Project and Development Finance Expert, with over 10 years of experience and expertise in organizational development, project consultancy, financial operations, and corporate governance.

items.title: Mid-Year Budget: Gov’t will present true state of economy, not hype – Gbande
items.description: Deputy Director of Operations at the Presidency, Mustapha Gbande, says the 2026 Mid-Year Budget Review will present an honest account of the country’s economic situation. He explained that the Mahama administration will not sugarcoat the challenges confronting the economy. His comments come ahead of Finance Minister Dr Cassiel Ato Forson’s presentation of the 2026 Mid-Year Budget Review to Parliament on Thursday, July 23, during which he is also expected to unveil the government’s much-anticipated New Economic Policy. Speaking ahead of the budget presentation, Mr Gbande said Ghanaians should expect a realistic assessment of the economy rather than exaggerated claims about its performance. “Expect a real briefing of the state of the economy because President Mahama’s government is not a hype government. It’s not a government that will come and give you information that doesn’t reflect reality,” he said. According to him, although the government has adopted strict expenditure controls that have slowed the payment of some obligations, the measures are necessary to stabilise the economy and prevent a repeat of what he described as years of fiscal mismanagement. “It’s a hardline approach, a very uncomfortable one. Bills are not being paid; contractors, most of them, are not getting their money. There is what they call front-loading of expenditure and all sorts of things. This has been done so that you and I will not feel the impact of the mismanagement,” he stated. Mr Gbande acknowledged that the country continues to face significant economic difficulties but maintained that the government is committed to confronting those challenges with transparency. “The reality is that things are hard. Things are tough. We find ourselves in difficult times,” he said. He added that the Mid-Year Budget Review would provide a comprehensive update on the state of the economy and outline the government’s expenditure plans as it seeks to maintain fiscal discipline while supporting economic recovery.

items.title: Owner of South Korea Michelin-starred restaurant faces jail for ant sorbet
items.description: The owner of a South Korean Michelin-starred restaurant is facing one year in jail for garnishing a palate-cleansing sorbet with black ants “to add a sour taste”, local media said. Seoul prosecutors requested the prison term for the anonymous owner of the unnamed eatery at a Monday court hearing for violating food safety regulations, the reports said. Under South Korean law, only 10 insect species are approved for human consumption, including grasshoppers and brown mealworm larvae. But the restaurant, which holds two prized Michelin stars, is accused of serving the forbidden insects imported from Thailand and the US, a food safety ministry statement said. Local media said the ants were served atop a sorbet. Authorities launched their probe last year, according to a food ministry statement, claiming the chef topped the icy desserts with “three to five” ants “to add a sour taste”. He made around 12,000 sales worth 120 million won ($81,000) over nearly four years until January 2025, the statement said. One evidence photograph released by the ministry showed a can labelled “Cuisine Black Ants” in a round tin. In addition to the prison term, prosecutors asked the Seoul Western District Court to issue a 20 million won fine, local outlet Newsis News Agency reported. The defendant’s lawyers argued the ant-topped confections were served only to customers who had consented in advance, the outlet said. “I apologise for this incident, which arose from my failure to fully understand the relevant regulations,” the defendant reportedly said in his closing statement. The court is due to deliver its ruling in early September.

items.title: Ancient Lebanese city of Tyre added to heritage danger list
items.description: Tyre, an ancient Phoenician city in southern Lebanon bombarded by Israel over recent months, was added Tuesday to the UNESCO list of world heritage sites in danger. The ancient city, founded some 4,700 years ago, is home to two protected sites listed as UNESCO World Heritage Sites, a Roman triumphal arch and a hippodrome dating from the second century.

items.title: US hits Iranian island, says war cost at $37.5 bn
items.description: The US military struck Iran's Larak Island in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, after President Donald Trump warned he was "not finished" with a war that has already cost Washington $37.5 billion. Critics have pointed to the cost of the war, which Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth said on Tuesday had soared to $37.5 billion, defending a request for tens of billions in additional funding for the military.

items.title: Athlétisme: Marthe Koala remporte la première place World Continental Tour Silver
items.description: L’athlète burkinabè Marthe Koala a remporté la première place au World Continental Tour Silver en Slovaquie, ce mercredi 22 juillet 2026. Elle vient encore d’ajouter une autre médaille d’or à son palmarès. «1ʳᵉ place au World Continental Tour Silver TIPOS P-T-S 2026 en Slovaquie avec une performance de 6m71. Fière de représenter le Burkina Faso avec honneur et détermination», a posté Marthe Koala sir sa page Facebook, ce mercredi 22 juillet 2026. Après avoir remercié «tous ceux qui croient» en elle et l'accompagnent dans cette aventure, Marthe Koala a déclaré que «ce n'est pas une ligne d'arrivée» mais «une nouvelle impulsion vers de plus grands objectifs» qui a été accomplie aujourd’hui en Slovaquie. En rappel, Marthe Koala s’était imposée au World athletics Continental Tour Gold, tournoi de Delden en Hollande avec une performance de 6,77 mètres le 21 juin dernier. Par Bernard BOUGOUM L’article Athlétisme: Marthe Koala remporte la première place World Continental Tour Silver est apparu en premier sur Wakat Séra .

items.title: Service-Wide Vote not slush fund, APC lectures Atiku
items.description: By Omeiza Ajayi ABUJA: The ruling All Progressives Congress APC has faulted former Vice President Atiku Abubakar over his allegation that the 2026 budgetary Service-Wide Vote, SWV, was set aside to fund President Bola Tinubu’s 2027 reelection campaign, describing the claim as either mischievous or a reflection of ignorance of basic public finance principles. In a statement issued in Abuja on Wednesday, the party said the Presidential Candidate of the African Democratic Congress ADC had become more disoriented and misguided than ever before in the history of his presidential ambitions, adding that Atiku advanced no facts to support his claim beyond conjecture calculated to inflame and mislead Nigerians. APC explained that a Service-Wide Vote is a long-established budgetary mechanism designed to cater for government-wide obligations and unforeseen expenditures that arise after the annual budget has been passed, and not a secret fund or illegal slush account as alleged by Atiku. “You would think that a former Vice President, a man with the longest-standing ambition to be president of Nigeria, would understand the concept and instrument of a ‘Service-Wide Vote’, an elementary principle of public finance. A Service-Wide Vote is a long-established and widely used budgetary mechanism designed to cater for government-wide obligations and unforeseen expenditures that arise after the annual budget has been passed. Similar contingency arrangements exist in virtually all major economies and democracies of the world. A Service-Wide Vote is not a secret fund or an illegal slush account, as Atiku has baselessly and recklessly alleged,” said party spokesman, Felix Morka. The APC spokesman said Atiku’s own record in government made his attempt to lecture Nigerians on fiscal responsibility ironic, describing his public service history as mired in corruption and profligacy. “As a serial presidential contestant, Atiku has never once articulated a clear vision or coherent plan for Nigeria’s development but has remained a petty peddler of fake news and doomsday narratives. Spewing baseless and inflammatory allegations without justification is utterly irresponsible and unbecoming of a former Vice President,” he said. APC listed several interventions he said demonstrated the Tinubu administration’s fiscal responsibility, including the Nigerian Education Loan Fund NELFUND, which he said has enabled close to two million students to access higher education, the implementation of the new national minimum wage, the Consumer Credit Scheme, and the Presidential Compressed Natural Gas CNG Initiative aimed at reducing transportation costs. It added that the administration had also rolled out food and other palliatives for vulnerable households, alongside increased investments in infrastructure, healthcare, agriculture, and industrial expansion to create jobs, as well as tax reforms designed to ease the burden on low-income Nigerians. According to the party, the Tinubu administration remained focused on the Renewed Hope Agenda, rebuilding the economy and deepening institutional reforms. The post Service-Wide Vote not slush fund, APC lectures Atiku appeared first on Vanguard News .
